text.Rotherham: and Adaiah, son of Jeroham, son of Pashhur, son of Malchijah,—and Maasai son of Adiel, son of Jahzerah, son of Meshullam, son of Meshillemith, son of Immer;
text.KJV: 9:12 And Adaiah the sonne of Ieroham, the sonne of Passhur, the sonne of Malchiiah, and Maasia the sonne of Adiel, the sonne of Iahzerah, the sonne of Meshullam, the sonne of Meshillemith, the sonne of Immer.
text.DRV: 9:12 Moreover Adaias the son of Jeroham, the son of Phassur, the son of Melchias: and Maasai the son of Adiel, the son of Jezra, the son of Mosollam, the son of Mosollamith, the son of Emmer.

text.NRSV: besides their kindred, heads of their ancestral houses, one thousand seven hundred sixty, qualified for the work of the service of the house of God.
text.Rotherham: and their brethren, chief men of their ancestral house, a thousand and seven hundred and sixty,—able men, for the business of the service of the house of God.
text.KJV: 9:13 And their brethren, heads of the house of their fathers, a thousand, and seuen hundred and threescore, very able men for the worke of the seruice of the house of God.
text.DRV: 9:13 their brethren also Princes by their families a thousand seven hundred three score, most able men of strength to the work of the ministry in the house of God.

text.NRSV: Of the Levites: Shemaiah son of Hasshub, son of Azrikam, son of Hashabiah, of the sons of Merari;
text.Rotherham: And, of the Levites, Shemaiah, son of Hasshub, son of Azrikam, son of Hashabiah, of the sons of Merari;
text.KJV: 9:14 And of the Leuites: Shemaiah the sonne of Hasshub, the sonne of Azrikam, the sonne of Hashabiah, of the sonnes of Merari.
text.DRV: 9:14 And of the Levites: Semeia the son of Hassub the son of Ezricam, the son of Hasebia of the sons of Merari.

text.NRSV: and Bakbakkar, Heresh, Galal, and Mattaniah son of Mica, son of Zichri, son of Asaph;
text.Rotherham: and Bakbakkar, Heresh, and Galal,—and Mattaniah, son of Mica, son of Zichri, son of Asaph;
text.KJV: 9:15 And Bakbakkar, Heresh, and Galal: and Mattaniah the sonne of Micah, the sonne of Zichri, the sonne of Asaph.
text.DRV: 9:15 Bacbacar also a carpenter, and Galal, and Mathania the son of Micha, the son of Zechri the son of Asaph:

text.NRSV: and Obadiah son of Shemaiah, son of Galal, son of Jeduthun, and Berechiah son of Asa, son of Elkanah, who lived in the villages of the Netophathites.
text.Rotherham: and Obadiah, son of Shemaiah, son of Galal, son of Jeduthun,—and Berechiah son of Asa, son of Elkanah, who dwelt in the villages of the Netophathites.
text.KJV: 9:16 And Obadiah the sonne of Shemaiah, the sonne of Galal, the sonne of Ieduthun: and Berechiah the sonne of Asa, the sonne of Elkanah, that dwelt in the villages of the Netophathites.
text.DRV: 9:16 and Obdia the son of Semei, the son of Galal, the son of Idithun: and Barachia the son of Asa, the son of Elcana, who dwelt in the courts of Netophati.

text.NRSV: The gatekeepers were: Shallum, Akkub, Talmon, Ahiman, and their kindred (Shallum was the chief),
text.Rotherham: And, the keepers of the gates, were Shallum, and Akkub, and Talmon, and Ahiman,—and their brethren—Shallum the chief;
text.KJV: 9:17 And the Porters were Shallum, and Akkub, and Talmon, and Ahiman, and their brethren: Shallum was the chiefe.
text.DRV: 9:17 And the porters: Sellum, and Accub, and Telmon, and Ahimam: and their brother Sellum the Prince,

text.NRSV: stationed until now at the King’s Gate on the east side. These were the gatekeepers of the camp of the Levites.
text.Rotherham: and, hitherto, they were in the gate of the king, eastward,—the same, were the keepers of the gate, for the camps of the sons of Levi.
text.KJV: 9:18 (Who hitherto waited in the kings gate Eastward) they were Porters in the companies of the children of Leui.
text.DRV: 9:18 until that time, in the king's gate toward the east, waited by their courses of the children of Levi.

text.NRSV: Shallum son of Kore, son of Ebiasaph, son of Korah, and his kindred of his ancestral house, the Korahites, were in charge of the work of the service, guardians of the thresholds of the tent, as their ancestors had been in charge of the camp of the Lord, guardians of the entrance.
text.Rotherham: And, Shallum, son of Kore, son of Ebiasaph, son of Korah and his brethren of his ancestral house—the Korahites, were over the business of the service, watchers at the vestibule of the tent,—and, their fathers, had been over the camp of Yahweh, watchers at the entrance.
text.KJV: 9:19 And Shallum the sonne of Kore, the sonne of Ebiasaph, the sonne of Korah, and his brethren (of the house of his father) the Korahites, were ouer the worke of the seruice, keepers of the gates of the Tabernacle: and their fathers being ouer the hoste of the Lord, were keepers of the entrie.
text.DRV: 9:19 But Sellum the son of Core the son of Abiasaph, the son of Core, with his brethren, and his father's house, these are the Corites over the works of the ministry, keepers of the entrances of the tabernacle: and their families in course keeping the entrance of the camp of our Lord.

text.NRSV: And Phinehas son of Eleazar was chief over them in former times; the Lord was with him.
text.Rotherham: And, Phinehas son of Eleazar, was, chief ruler, over them aforetime, Yahweh, being with him.
text.KJV: 9:20 And Phinehas the sonne of Eleazar was the ruler ouer them in time past, and the Lord was with him.
text.DRV: 9:20 And Phinees the son of Eleasar, was their Prince before our Lord.

text.NRSV: Zechariah son of Meshelemiah was gatekeeper at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: Zechariah son of Meshelemiah, was door-keeper at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.KJV: 9:21 And Zechariah the sonne of Meshelemiah, was porter of the doore of the Tabernacle of the Congregation.
text.DRV: 9:21 Moreover Zacharias the son of Mosollamia, porter of the gate of the tabernacle of testimony.

text.NRSV: All these, who were chosen as gatekeepers at the thresholds, were two hundred twelve. They were enrolled by genealogies in their villages. These David and the seer Samuel established in their office of trust.
text.Rotherham: All those who were chosen for door-keepers in the vestibule, were two hundred and twelve,—the same, in their villages, had registered themselves, the same, did David and Samuel the seer establish in their trust.
text.KJV: 9:22 All these which were chosen to be porters in the gates, were two hundred and twelue. These were reckoned by their genealogie in their villages: whom Dauid and Samuel the Seer, did ordeine in their set office.
text.DRV: 9:22 All these chosen men for porters, at every gate, two hundred twelve: and appointed out in their proper towns: Whom David and Samuel the seer appointed, upon their fidelity.

text.NRSV: So they and their sons were in charge of the gates of the house of the Lord, that is, the house of the tent, as guards.
text.Rotherham: So, they and their sons, were over the gates of the house of Yahweh, of the house of the tent, by watches.
text.KJV: 9:23 So they and their children had the ouersight of the gates of the house of the Lord, namely, the house of the Tabernacle, by wards.
text.DRV: 9:23 as well them as their sons, in the doors of the house of our Lord, and in the tabernacle by their courses.

text.NRSV: The gatekeepers were on the four sides, east, west, north, and south;
text.Rotherham: Towards the four winds, were the keepers of the gates,—eastward, westward, northward, and southward.
text.KJV: 9:24 In foure quarters were the porters: toward the East, West, North, and South.
text.DRV: 9:24 By the four winds were the porters: that is to say, toward the East, and toward the West, & toward the North, & toward the South.

text.NRSV: and their kindred who were in their villages were obliged to come in every seven days, from time to time, to be with these;
text.Rotherham: And, their brethren in their villages, had to come in, every seven days, from time to time, along with these.
text.KJV: 9:25 And their brethren, which were in their villages, were to come after seuen dayes, from time to time with them.
text.DRV: 9:25 And their brethren dwelt in villages, & came upon their Sabbaths from time to time.

text.NRSV: for the four chief gatekeepers, who were Levites, were in charge of the chambers and the treasures of the house of God.
text.Rotherham: For, in trust, were four mighty men of the keepers of the gates, the same, were Levites,—and they were over the chambers, and over the treasuries of the house of God.
text.KJV: 9:26 For these Leuites, the foure chiefe porters, were in their set office, and were ouer the chambers and treasuries of the house of God.
text.DRV: 9:26 To these four Levites was committed all the number of porters, & they were over the chambers, and treasures of the house of our Lord.

text.NRSV: And they would spend the night near the house of God; for on them lay the duty of watching, and they had charge of opening it every morning.
text.Rotherham: And, round about the house of God, used they to lodge,—for, upon them, was the charge, and they were over the setting open, morning by morning.
text.KJV: 9:27 ¶ And they lodged round about the house of God, because the charge was vpon them, and the opening thereof euery morning, perteined to them.
text.DRV: 9:27 Also round about the temple of our Lord they abode in their watches: that when it was time, they in the morning might open the doors.

text.NRSV: Some of them had charge of the utensils of service, for they were required to count them when they were brought in and taken out.
text.Rotherham: And, some from among them, were over the utensils of the service,—for, by number, used they to bring them in, and, by number, used they to take them forth.
text.KJV: 9:28 And certaine of them had the charge of the ministring vessels, that they should bring them in and out by tale.
text.DRV: 9:28 Of these men's stock there were also over the vessels of the ministry: for by number the vessels were both brought in and carried out.

text.NRSV: Others of them were appointed over the furniture, and over all the holy utensils, also over the choice flour, the wine, the oil, the incense, and the spices.
text.Rotherham: And, some from among them, were appointed over the utensils, yea over all the vessels of the holy place,—and over the fine meal, and the wine, and the oil, and the frankincense, and the spices.
text.KJV: 9:29 Some of them also were appointed to ouersee the vessels, and all the instruments of the Sanctuarie, and the fine floure, and the wine, and the oyle, and the frankincense, and the spices.
text.DRV: 9:29 Of them also they that had the implements of the sanctuary committed unto them, did oversee the flour, and wine, and oil, and frankincense, and spices.

text.NRSV: Others, of the sons of the priests, prepared the mixing of the spices,
text.Rotherham: And, some from among the sons of the priests, were compounders of perfumes, with the spices.
text.KJV: 9:30 And some of the sonnes of the Priests made the oyntment of the spices.
text.DRV: 9:30 And the sons of Priests made ointments of the spices.

text.NRSV: and Mattithiah, one of the Levites, the firstborn of Shallum the Korahite, was in charge of making the flat cakes.
text.Rotherham: And, Mattithiah, from among the Levites,—the same, was the firstborn of Shallum the Korahite—was in trust over the making of the flat cakes.
text.KJV: 9:31 And Mattithiah, one of the Leuites (who was the first borne of Shallum the Korahite) had the set office ouer the things that were made in the pannes.
text.DRV: 9:31 And Mathathias a Levite the first-begotten of Sellum the Corite, was overseer of those things, which were fried in the frying pan.

text.NRSV: Also some of their kindred of the Kohathites had charge of the rows of bread, to prepare them for every sabbath.
text.Rotherham: And, some from among the Kohathites, of their brethren, were over the Bread that was set in Array,—to place it sabbath by sabbath.
text.KJV: 9:32 And other of their brethren of the sonnes of the Kohathites, were ouer the Shew-bread to prepare it euery Sabbath.
text.DRV: 9:32 Moreover of the children of Caath their brethren, there were over the loaves of proposition, to prepare always new every Sabbath.

text.NRSV: Now these are the singers, the heads of ancestral houses of the Levites, living in the chambers of the temple free from other service, for they were on duty day and night.
text.Rotherham: These, also were the singers, ancestral chiefs of the Levites, in the chambers, free,—for, by day and by night, was there [a charge] upon them, in the business.
text.KJV: 9:33 And these are the singers, chiefe of the fathers of the Leuites, who remayning in the chambers, were free: for they were imployed in that worke, day and night.
text.DRV: 9:33 These are the chief of the singing men by the families of the Levites, which abode in the chambers, that they might day and night continually serve in their ministry.

text.NRSV: These were heads of ancestral houses of the Levites, according to their generations, chiefs; these lived in Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: These, were the ancestral chiefs, of the Levites, by their generations, chief men,—these, dwelt in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 9:34 These chiefe fathers of the Leuites, were chiefe throughout their generations; these dwelt at Ierusalem.
text.DRV: 9:34 The heads of the Levites, Princes in their families, tarried in Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: In Gibeon lived the father of Gibeon, Jeiel, and the name of his wife was Maacah.
text.Rotherham: And, in Gibeon, dwelt the father of Gibeon, Jeiel,—the name of whose wife, was Maacah:
text.KJV: 9:35 ¶ And in Gibeon dwelt the father of Gibeon, Iehiel, whose wiues name was Maacha:
text.DRV: 9:35 And in Gabaon abode Jehiel the father of Gabaon, and the name of his wife Maacha.

text.NRSV: His firstborn son was Abdon, then Zur, Kish, Baal, Ner, Nadab,
text.Rotherham: and, his firstborn son, Abdon,—and Zur, and Kish, and Baal and Ner, and Nadab;
text.KJV: 9:36 And his first borne sonne Abdon, then Zur, and Kish, and Baal, and Ner, and Nadab,
text.DRV: 9:36 His first-begotten son Abdon, and Sur, and Cis, and Baal, and Ner, and Nadab,

text.NRSV: Gedor, Ahio, Zechariah, and Mikloth;
text.Rotherham: and Gedor, and Ahio, and Zechariah, and Mikloh.
text.KJV: 9:37 And Gedor, and Ahio, and Zechariah, and Mikloth.
text.DRV: 9:37 Gedor also, and Ahio, and Zacharias, and Macelloth.

text.NRSV: and Mikloth became the father of Shimeam; and these also lived opposite their kindred in Jerusalem, with their kindred.
text.Rotherham: And, Mikloth, begat Shimeam—and, they also, over against their brethren, did dwell in Jerusalem, along with their brethren.
text.KJV: 9:38 And Mikloth begate Shimeam: and they also dwelt with their brethren at Ierusalem, ouer against their brethren.
text.DRV: 9:38 Moreover Macelloth begat Samaan, these dwelt over against their brethren in Jerusalem, with their brethren.

text.NRSV: Ner became the father of Kish, Kish became the father of Saul, Saul became the father of Jonathan, Malchi-shua, Abinadab, and Esh-baal;
text.Rotherham: And, Ner, begat Kith, and, Kish, begat Saul,—and, Saul, begat Jonathan, and Malchishua, and Abinadab, and Eshbaal;
text.KJV: 9:39 And Ner begat Kish, and Kish begate Saul, and Saul begate Ionathan, and Malchishua, and Abinadab, and Eshbaal.
text.DRV: 9:39 And Ner begat Cis: and Cis begat Saul: and Saul begat Jonathas, and Melchisua, and Abinadab, and Esbaal.

text.NRSV: and the son of Jonathan was Merib-baal; and Merib-baal became the father of Micah.
text.Rotherham: and, the son of Jonathan, was Merib-baal,—and, Merib-baal, begat Micah;
text.KJV: 9:40 And the sonne of Ionathan was Meribbaal: and Meribbaal begate Micah.
text.DRV: 9:40 And the son of Jonathas, Meribbaal: & Meribbaal begat Micha.

text.NRSV: The sons of Micah: Pithon, Melech, Tahrea, and Ahaz;
text.Rotherham: and, the sons of Micah, were Pithon, and Melech, and Tahrea [and Ahaz] ;
text.KJV: 9:41 And the sonnes of Micah were Pithon, and Melech, and Tahrea, and Ahaz.
text.DRV: 9:41 Moreover the sons of Micha, Phithon, & Melech, & Tharaa, and Ahaz.

text.NRSV: and Ahaz became the father of Jarah, and Jarah became the father of Alemeth, Azmaveth, and Zimri; and Zimri became the father of Moza.
text.Rotherham: and, Ahaz, begat Jarah, and, Jarah begat Alemeth, and Azmaveth, and Zimri,—and Zimri, begat Moza;
text.KJV: 9:42 And Ahaz begate Iarah, and Iarah begate Alemeth, & Azmaueth, and Zimri: and Zimri begate Moza,
text.DRV: 9:42 And Ahaz begat Jara, & Jara begat Alamath, & Azmoth, & Zamri. And Zamri begat Mosa.

text.NRSV: Moza became the father of Binea; and Rephaiah was his son, Eleasah his son, Azel his son.
text.Rotherham: and, Moza, begat Binea,—and Raphaiah his son, Eleasah his son, Azel his son;
text.KJV: 9:43 And Moza begate Binea: and Rephaiah his son, Eleasah his sonne, Azel his sonne.
text.DRV: 9:43 And Mosa begat Banaa: whose son Raphaia begat Elasa: of whom was born Asel.

text.NRSV: Azel had six sons, and these are their names: Azrikam, Bocheru, Ishmael, Sheariah, Obadiah, and Hanan; these were the sons of Azel.
text.Rotherham: and, Azel, had six sons, and these are their names—Azrikam, [his firstborn], and Ishmael, and Sheariah and Obadiah, and Hanan, [and Asah] . These, were the sons of Azel.
text.KJV: 9:44 And Azel had sixe sonnes, whose names are these: Azrikam, Bocheru, and Ismael, and Sheariah, and Obadiah, and Hanan. These were the sonnes of Azel.
text.DRV: 9:44 Moreover Asel had six sons of these names, Ezricam, Bochru, Ismael, Saria, Obdia, Hanan, these are the sons of Asel.

text.NRSV: Now the Philistines fought against Israel; and the men of Israel fled before the Philistines, and fell slain on Mount Gilboa.
text.Rotherham: Now, the Philistines, fought against Israel,—and the men of Israel fled from before the Philistines, and the wounded fell in Mount Gilboa.
text.KJV: 10:1 Nowe the Philistines fought against Israel, and the men of Israel fled from before the Philistines, and fell downe slaine in mount Gilboa.
text.DRV: 10:1 And the Philisthiims fought against Israel, and the men of Israel fled from the Palesthines, and they fell wounded in mount Gelboe.

text.NRSV: The Philistines overtook Saul and his sons; and the Philistines killed Jonathan and Abinadab and Malchi-shua, sons of Saul.
text.Rotherham: And the Philistines followed hard after Saul, and after his sons,—and the Philistines smote Jonathan, and Abinadab, and Malchishua, sons of Saul.
text.KJV: 10:2 And the Philistines followed hard after Saul, and after his sonnes, and the Philistines slew Ionathan, and Abinadab, and Malchishua, the sonnes of Saul.
text.DRV: 10:2 And when the Philisthians were come near pursuing Saul and his sons, they struck Jonathas, and Abinadab, and Melchisua the sons of Saul.

text.NRSV: The battle pressed hard upon Saul; and the archers found him, and he was wounded by the archers.
text.Rotherham: And the battle, went sore, against Saul, and the archers discovered him with the bow,—and he was terrified because of the archers.
text.KJV: 10:3 And the battell went sore against Saul, and the archers hit him, and he was wounded of the archers.
text.DRV: 10:3 And the battle grew sore against Saul, and the archers found him, and wounded him with arrows.

text.NRSV: Then Saul said to his armor-bearer, “Draw your sword, and thrust me through with it, so that these uncircumcised may not come and make sport of me.” But his armor-bearer was unwilling, for he was terrified. So Saul took his own sword and fell on it.
text.Rotherham: Therefore said Saul unto his armour-bearer, Draw out thy sword and thrust me through therewith, lest these uncircumcised come and abuse me. But his armour-bearer would not, for he feared greatly,—so then Saul took the sword, and fell upon it.
text.KJV: 10:4 Then saide Saul to his armour bearer, Draw thy sword, and thrust me through therewith, lest these vncircumcised come, and abuse mee: but his armour bearer would not, for he was sore afraid. So Saul tooke a sword, and fell vpon it.
text.DRV: 10:4 And Saul said to his esquire: Draw thy sword, and kill me: lest perhaps these uncircumcised come, and deride me. But his harness-bearer would not, being frighted with fear: Saul therefore caught his sword, and fell upon it.

text.NRSV: When his armor-bearer saw that Saul was dead, he also fell on his sword and died.
text.Rotherham: And, when his armour-bearer saw that Saul was dead, then, he also, fell upon the sword, and died.
text.KJV: 10:5 And when his armour bearer saw that Saul was dead, hee fell likewise on the sword, and died.
text.DRV: 10:5 Which when his harness-bearer had seen, to wit, that Saul was dead, himself also fell upon his sword, and died.

text.NRSV: Thus Saul died; he and his three sons and all his house died together.
text.Rotherham: So Saul died, and his three sons, and, all his house, together, died.
text.KJV: 10:6 So Saul died, and his three sonnes, and all his house died together.
text.DRV: 10:6 Saul therefore died, and his three sons, and all his house fell together.

text.NRSV: When all the men of Israel who were in the valley saw that the army had fled and that Saul and his sons were dead, they abandoned their towns and fled; and the Philistines came and occupied them.
text.Rotherham: And, when all the men of Israel that were in the vale saw, that they had fled, and that Saul and his sons were dead, then forsook they their cities, and fled, and the Philistines came and dwelt in them.
text.KJV: 10:7 And when all the men of Israel that were in the valley, saw that they fled, and that Saul and his sonnes were dead: then they forsooke their cities, and fled, and the Philistines came and dwelt in them.
text.DRV: 10:7 Which when the men of Israel had seen that dwelt in the champion, they fled: and Saul and his sons being dead, they forsook their cities, and were dispersed hither and thither: and the Philisthiims came, and dwelt in them.

text.NRSV: The next day when the Philistines came to strip the dead, they found Saul and his sons fallen on Mount Gilboa.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, on the morrow, when the Philistines came to strip the slain,—that they found Saul, and his sons, lying prostrate in Mount Gilboa.
text.KJV: 10:8 ¶ And it came to passe on the morrow, when the Philistines came to strip the slaine, that they found Saul and his sonnes fallen in mount Gilboa.
text.DRV: 10:8 The next day therefore the Philisthiims taking away the spoils of them that were slain, found Saul and his sons lying on mount Gelboe.

text.NRSV: They stripped him and took his head and his armor, and sent messengers throughout the land of the Philistines to carry the good news to their idols and to the people.
text.Rotherham: So they stript him, and took his head, and his armour,—and sent throughout the land of the Philistines round about, to publish it to their idols, and to the people.
text.KJV: 10:9 And when they had stripped him, they tooke his head, and his armour, and sent into the land of the Philistines round about, to cary tidings vnto their idoles, and to the people.
text.DRV: 10:9 And when they had spoiled him, and cut off his head, and stripped him of his armour, they sent into their land, that it should be carried about, & should be shewed in the temples of the idols, and to the people:

text.NRSV: They put his armor in the temple of their gods, and fastened his head in the temple of Dagon.
text.Rotherham: And they put his armour, in the house of their gods,—but, his skull, fastened they up, in the house of Dagon.
text.KJV: 10:10 And they put his armour in the house of their gods, and fastened his head in the temple of Dagon.
text.DRV: 10:10 & his armour they dedicated in the temple of their god, and the head they nailed up in the temple of Dagon.

text.NRSV: But when all Jabesh-gilead heard everything that the Philistines had done to Saul,
text.Rotherham: And, when all Jabesh-Gilead heard all that the Philistines had done to Saul,
text.KJV: 10:11 ¶ And when all Iabesh Gilead heard all that the Philistines had done to Saul:
text.DRV: 10:11 When the men of Jabes Galaad had heard this, to wit, all things that the Philisthiims had done upon Saul,

text.NRSV: all the valiant warriors got up and took the body of Saul and the bodies of his sons, and brought them to Jabesh. Then they buried their bones under the oak in Jabesh, and fasted seven days.
text.Rotherham: then rose up all the men of valour, and took away the corpse of Saul, and the corpses of his sons, and brought them to Jabesh,—and buried their bodies under the terebinth, in Jabesh, and fasted seven days.
text.KJV: 10:12 They arose, all the valiant men, and tooke away the body of Saul, and the bodies of his sonnes, and brought them to Iabesh, and buried their bones vnder the oke in Iabesh, and fasted seuen dayes.
text.DRV: 10:12 every one of the valiant men arose, and took the bodies of Saul and of his sons, and brought them into Jabes, & buried their bones under an oak, that was in Jabes, and they fasted seven days.

text.NRSV: So Saul died for his unfaithfulness; he was unfaithful to the Lord in that he did not keep the command of the Lord; moreover, he had consulted a medium, seeking guidance,
text.Rotherham: Thus died Saul, for his unfaithfulness wherewith he dealt unfaithfully against Yahweh, over the word of Yahweh, which he kept not,—and, also, in asking by necromancy when he desired to enquire;
text.KJV: 10:13 ¶ So Saul died for his transgression which hee committed against the Lord, euen against the word of the Lord which he kept not, and also for asking counsel of one that had a familiar spirit, to enquire of it:
text.DRV: 10:13 Saul therefore died for his iniquities, for that he transgressed the commandment of our Lord which he had commanded, and kept it not: yea and besides also consulted the Pythoness,

text.NRSV: and did not seek guidance from the Lord. Therefore the Lord put him to death and turned the kingdom over to David son of Jesse.
text.Rotherham: and enquired not of Yahweh, wherefore he slew him,—and turned round the kingdom unto David, son of Jesse.
text.KJV: 10:14 And enquired not of the Lord: therefore he slew him, and turned the kingdome vnto Dauid the sonne of Iesse.
text.DRV: 10:14 and trusted not in our Lord: for the which he slew him, and transferred his Kingdom to David the son of Isai.

text.NRSV: Then all Israel gathered together to David at Hebron and said, “See, we are your bone and flesh.
text.Rotherham: Then did all Israel gather themselves together to David, unto Hebron, saying,—Lo! thy bone and thy flesh, are we.
text.KJV: 11:1 Then all Israel gathered themselues to Dauid vnHebron, saying, Behold, wee are thy bone and thy flesh.
text.DRV: 11:1 All Israel therefore was gathered together to David in Hebron, saying: We are thy bone, and thy flesh.

text.NRSV: For some time now, even while Saul was king, it was you who commanded the army of Israel. The Lord your God said to you: It is you who shall be shepherd of my people Israel, you who shall be ruler over my people Israel.”
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, in time past even when Saul was king, thou, wast he that led out and brought in Israel,—and Yahweh thy God said unto thee, Thou, shalt shepherd my people Israel, Yea, thou, shalt be chief ruler, over my people Israel.
text.KJV: 11:2 And moreouer in time past, euen when Saul was king, thou wast he that leddest out and broughtest in Israel: and the Lord thy God said vnto thee, Thou shalt feede my people Israel, and thou shalt be ruler ouer my people Israel.
text.DRV: 11:2 Yesterday also, and the day before, when Saul as yet reigned, thou wast he that didst lead out and lead in Israel: for to thee our Lord thy God said: Thou shalt feed my people Israel, and thou shalt be Prince over it.

text.NRSV: So all the elders of Israel came to the king at Hebron, and David made a covenant with them at Hebron before the Lord. And they anointed David king over Israel, according to the word of the Lord by Samuel.
text.Rotherham: Thus came all the elders of Israel unto the king, to Hebron, and David solemnised to them a covenant in Hebron, before Yahweh,—and they anointed David to be king over Israel, according to the word of Yahweh, through Samuel.
text.KJV: 11:3 Therefore came all the Elders of Israel to the king to Hebron, and Dauid made a couenant with them in Hebron before the Lord, and they annointed Dauid king ouer Israel, according to the word of the Lord, by Samuel.
text.DRV: 11:3 All the ancients therefore of Israel came to the king into Hebron, and David entered into a league with them before our Lord: and they anointed him king over Israel, according to the word of our Lord, which he spake in the hand of Samuel.

text.NRSV: David and all Israel went to Jerusalem, that is Jebus, where the Jebusites were, the inhabitants of the land.
text.Rotherham: Then went David and all Israel to Jerusalem, the same, was Jebus,—where were the Jebusites, the inhabitants of the land.
text.KJV: 11:4 ¶ And Dauid & all Israel, went to Ierusalem, which is Iebus, where the Iebusites were the inhabitants of the land.
text.DRV: 11:4 David also went, and all Israel into Jerusalem. This is Jebus, where the Jebuseites were the inhabiters of the land.

text.NRSV: The inhabitants of Jebus said to David, “You will not come in here.” Nevertheless David took the stronghold of Zion, now the city of David.
text.Rotherham: And the inhabitants of Jebus said unto David, Thou shalt not come in hither,—But David captured the citadel of Zion, the same, is the city of David.
text.KJV: 11:5 And the inhabitants of Iebus said to Dauid, Thou shalt not come hither. Neuerthelesse Dauid tooke the castle of Zion, which is the citie of Dauid.
text.DRV: 11:5 And they that dwelt in Jebus, said to David: Thou shalt not come in here. Moreover David took the tower of Sion, which is the City of David,

text.NRSV: David had said, “Whoever strikes the Jebusites first shall be chief and commander.” And Joab son of Zeruiah went up first, so he became chief.
text.Rotherham: And David said, Whosoever smiteth the Jebusites, first, shall become a chief, and a ruler,—So then Joab son of Zeruiah, went up first, and became a chief.
text.KJV: 11:6 And Dauid said, Whosoeuer smiteth the Iebusites first, shall be chiefe, and captaine. So Ioab the sonne of Zeruiah went first vp, and was chiefe.
text.DRV: 11:6 & he said: Every one that shall among the first strike the Jebusite, shall be the Prince & chief captain. Joab therefore the son of Sarvia went up first, and was made the Prince.

text.NRSV: David resided in the stronghold; therefore it was called the city of David.
text.Rotherham: And David dwelt in the citadel,—for this cause, did they call it, The City of David.
text.KJV: 11:7 And Dauid dwelt in the castell: therefore they called it the citie of Dauid.
text.DRV: 11:7 And David dwelt in the tower, and therefore it was called the City of David.

text.NRSV: He built the city all around, from the Millo in complete circuit; and Joab restored the rest of the city.
text.Rotherham: And he built the city round about, from Millo even as far as the circuit,—but, Joab, suffered the remainder of the city to live.
text.KJV: 11:8 And he built the citie round about, euen from Millo round about: and Ioab repaired the rest of the citie.
text.DRV: 11:8 And he built the City round about from Mello unto a round compass, and Joab built the rest of the city.

text.NRSV: And David became greater and greater, for the Lord of hosts was with him.
text.Rotherham: Thus did David wax greater and yet greater,—and, Yahweh of Hosts, was with him.
text.KJV: 11:9 So Dauid waxed greater and greater: for the Lord of hostes was with him.
text.DRV: 11:9 And David prospered going and increasing, and the Lord of hosts was with him.

text.NRSV: Now these are the chiefs of David’s warriors, who gave him strong support in his kingdom, together with all Israel, to make him king, according to the word of the Lord concerning Israel.
text.Rotherham: Now, these, are the chiefs of the heroes, that pertained to David, who held strongly with him in his kingdom, with all Israel, to make him king,—according to the word of Yahweh, concerning Israel.
text.KJV: 11:10 ¶ These also are the chiefe of the mightie men, whom Dauid had, who strenthened themselues with him in his kingdome, and with all Israel, to make him king according to the word of the Lord, concerning Israel.
text.DRV: 11:10 These are the Princes of the valiant men of David, which holp him to be made king over all Israel according to the word of our Lord, which he spake to Israel

text.NRSV: This is the list of David’s warriors: Jashobeam son of a Hachmonite was chief of the Three; he wielded his spear against three hundred whom he killed at one time.
text.Rotherham: And, this, is the number of the heroes who pertained to David,—Jashobeam son of a Hachmonite, chief of the thirty, the same, brandished his spear over three hundred—slain at one time.
text.KJV: 11:11 And this is the number of the mightie men, whom Dauid had: Iashobeam an Hachmonite, the chiefe of the captaines: he lift vp his speare against three hundred, slaine by him at one time.
text.DRV: 11:11 And this is the number of David's strong ones: Jesbaam the son of Hachamoni Prince among thirty: this man lifted up his spear upon three hundred wounded at one time.

text.NRSV: And next to him among the three warriors was Eleazar son of Dodo, the Ahohite.
text.Rotherham: And, after him, was Eleazar son of Dodai, the Ahohite,—he, was among the three heroes;
text.KJV: 11:12 And after him was Eleazar the sonne of Dodo the Ahohite, who was one of the three mighties.
text.DRV: 11:12 And after him Eleazar his uncle's son an Ahohite, who was among the three mighties.

text.NRSV: He was with David at Pas-dammim when the Philistines were gathered there for battle. There was a plot of ground full of barley. Now the people had fled from the Philistines,
text.Rotherham: he, was with David in Pas-dammim, where, the Philistines, were gathered together to battle, and there was a field-plot full of barley,—and, the people, fled, from before the Philistines;
text.KJV: 11:13 He was with Dauid at Pasdammim; and there the Philistines were gathered together to battell, where was a parcell of ground full of barley, and the people fled from before the Philistines.
text.DRV: 11:13 This was with David in Phesdomim, when the Philisthiims were gathered to that place into battle: and the field of that country was full of barley, and the people was fled from the face of the Philisthiims.

text.NRSV: but he and David stood their ground in the middle of the plot, defended it, and killed the Philistines; and the Lord saved them by a great victory.
text.Rotherham: so they took their stand in the midst of the plot and rescued it, and smote the Philistines,—and Yahweh wrought a great victory.
text.KJV: 11:14 And they set themselues in the middest of that parcell, and deliuered it, and slue the Philistines, & the Lord saued them by a great deliuerance.
text.DRV: 11:14 These stood in the midst of the field, and defended him: and when they had strucken the Philistheans, our Lord gave great health to his people.

text.NRSV: Three of the thirty chiefs went down to the rock to David at the cave of Adullam, while the army of the Philistines was encamped in the valley of Rephaim.
text.Rotherham: And three of the thirty chiefs went down upon the rock, unto David, into the cave of Adullam,—a host of Philistines, being encamped in the vale of Rephaim.
text.KJV: 11:15 ¶ Now three of the thirtie captaines, went downe to the rocke of Dauid, into the caue of Adullam, and the host of the Philistines encamped in the valley of Rephaim.
text.DRV: 11:15 And there went down three of the thirty Princes to a rock, wherein David was, to the cave of Odollam, when the Philisthiims had camped in the Vale Raphaim.

text.NRSV: David was then in the stronghold; and the garrison of the Philistines was then at Bethlehem.
text.Rotherham: Now, David, then was in the stronghold,—and, a garrison of Philistines, then was in Bethlehem.
text.KJV: 11:16 And Dauid was then in the hold, and the Philistines garison was then at Bethlehem.
text.DRV: 11:16 Moreover David was in a hold, and the ward of the Philisthimes in Bethlehem.

text.NRSV: David said longingly, “O that someone would give me water to drink from the well of Bethlehem that is by the gate!”
text.Rotherham: And David longed, and said—Who will give me to drink of the water, out of the well of Bethlehem, that is within the gate?
text.KJV: 11:17 And Dauid longed and said, Oh that one would giue me drinke of the water of the well of Bethlehem, that is at the gate.
text.DRV: 11:17 David therefore desired and said: O that some man would give me water of the cistern of Bethlehem, which is in the gate.

text.NRSV: Then the Three broke through the camp of the Philistines, and drew water from the well of Bethlehem that was by the gate, and they brought it to David. But David would not drink of it; he poured it out to the Lord,
text.Rotherham: And the three brake through the host of the Philistines, and drew water out of the well of Bethlehem, that was within the gate, and bare it, and brought it in unto David,—yet would not David drink it, but poured it out unto Yahweh;
text.KJV: 11:18 And the three brake through the host of the Philistines, and drew water out of the well of Bethlehem, that was by the gate, and tooke it and brought it to Dauid. But Dauid would not drink of it, but powred it out to the Lord,
text.DRV: 11:18 These three therefore went forward through the midst of the camp of the Philisthiims, and drew water of the cistern of Bethlehem, which was in the gate, & brought it to David to drink: who would not, but rather offered it to our Lord,

text.NRSV: and said, “My God forbid that I should do this. Can I drink the blood of these men? For at the risk of their lives they brought it.” Therefore he would not drink it. The three warriors did these things.
text.Rotherham: and said—Far be it from me, of my God, that I should do this! The blood of these men, shall I drink, with their lives? For, with their lives, have they brought it! So he would not drink it. These things, did the three heroes.
text.KJV: 11:19 And said, My God forbid it mee, that I should doe this thing. Shall I drinke the blood of these men, that haue put their liues in ieopardie? for with the ieopardie of their liues, they brought it: therfore he would not drink it. These things did these three mightiest.
text.DRV: 11:19 saying: God forbid that in the sight of my God I should do this, and should drink the blood of these men: because in the peril of their lives they have brought me the water. And for this cause he would not drink. These things did the three most valiants.

text.NRSV: Now Abishai, the brother of Joab, was chief of the Thirty. With his spear he fought against three hundred and killed them, and won a name beside the Three.
text.Rotherham: And, Abishai brother of Joab—he, was the chief of three, he, having brandished his spear against three hundred—who were slain,—and so, he, had a name among three.
text.KJV: 11:20 ¶ And Abishai the brother of Ioab, he was chiefe of the three. For lifting vp his speare against three hundred, he slew them, and had a name among the three.
text.DRV: 11:20 Abisai also the brother of Joab he was Prince of three, and he lifted up his spear against three hundred wounded, and he was among three most renowned,

text.NRSV: He was the most renowned of the Thirty, and became their commander; but he did not attain to the Three.
text.Rotherham: Of the three in the second rank, was he honourable, therefore became he their captain,—though, unto the [first] three, did he not attain.
text.KJV: 11:21 Of the three, hee was more honourable then the two, for he was their captaine; howbeit, he attained not to the first three.
text.DRV: 11:21 and among the second three he the noble one, and Prince of them: but yet unto the three first he raught not.

text.NRSV: Benaiah son of Jehoiada was a valiant man of Kabzeel, a doer of great deeds; he struck down two sons of Ariel of Moab. He also went down and killed a lion in a pit on a day when snow had fallen.
text.Rotherham: Benaiah, son of Jehoiada, son of a man of valour, hero of many a deed, from Kabziel—he, smote the two [sons] of Ariel of Moab, he, also went down and smote a lion in the midst of a pit, in time of snow;
text.KJV: 11:22 Benaiah the sonne of Iehoiada, the sonne of a valiant man of Kabzeel, who had done many acts: he slue two Lyon-like men of Moab, also he went downe and slue a Lyon in a pit in a snowy day.
text.DRV: 11:22 Banaias the son of Joiada a most valiant man, of Cabseel: who had done many facts, he struck the two Ariel of Moab: & he went down, and slew the lion in the midst of the cistern in the time of snow.

text.NRSV: And he killed an Egyptian, a man of great stature, five cubits tall. The Egyptian had in his hand a spear like a weaver’s beam; but Benaiah went against him with a staff, snatched the spear out of the Egyptian’s hand, and killed him with his own spear.
text.Rotherham: he, also smote an Egyptian, a man of [great] stature, five cubits, and, in the hand of the Egyptian, was a spear like a weaver’s beam, so he went down unto him with a staff,—and wrested the spear out of the hand of the Egyptian, and slew him with his spear.
text.KJV: 11:23 And he slue an Egyptian, a man of great stature, fiue cubits high, and in the Egyptians hand was a speare like a weauers beame: and he went downe to him with a staffe, and pluckt the speare out of the Egyptians hand, and slue him with his owne speare.
text.DRV: 11:23 And he struck the Ægyptian, whose stature was of five cubits, and which had a spear as the weaver's beam: he therefore went down to him with a rod, and by force took away the spear that he held in his hand, and slew him with his own spear.

text.NRSV: Such were the things Benaiah son of Jehoiada did, and he won a name beside the three warriors.
text.Rotherham: These things, did Benaiah son of Jehoiada, so, he, had a name among three heroes.
text.KJV: 11:24 These things did Benaiah the sonne of Iehoiada, and had the name among the three mighties.
text.DRV: 11:24 These things did Banaias the son of Joiada, who was among the three valiants most renowned,

text.NRSV: He was renowned among the Thirty, but he did not attain to the Three. And David put him in charge of his bodyguard.
text.Rotherham: Among the thirty, lo! honourable, was he, but, unto the [first] three, did he not attain,—howbeit David set him over his council.
text.KJV: 11:25 Behold, hee was honourable among the thirtie, but attained not to the first three: and Dauid set him ouer his guard.
text.DRV: 11:25 the first among thirty, but yet the three he raught not: and David made him of his counsel.

text.NRSV: The warriors of the armies were Asahel brother of Joab, Elhanan son of Dodo of Bethlehem,
text.Rotherham: Now, the heroes of the forces, were,—Asahel, brother of Joab, Elhanan, son of Dodo, of Bethlehem;
text.KJV: 11:26 ¶ Also the valiant men of the armies were Asahel the brother of Ioab, Elhanan the sonne of Dodo of Bethlehem.
text.DRV: 11:26 Moreover the most valiant men in the army, Asahel the brother of Joab, and Elchanan the son of his uncle of Bethlehem,

text.NRSV: Shammoth of Harod, Helez the Pelonite,
text.Rotherham: Shammoth, the Harorite, Helez the Paltite;
text.KJV: 11:27 Shammoth the Harorite, Helez the Pelonite,
text.DRV: 11:27 Sammoth an Arorite, Helles a Phalonite,

text.NRSV: Ira son of Ikkesh of Tekoa, Abiezer of Anathoth,
text.Rotherham: Ira, son of Ikkesh, the Tekoite, Abiezer, the Anathothite;
text.KJV: 11:28 Ira the sonne of Ikkesh the Tekoite, Abiezer the Antothite,
text.DRV: 11:28 Ira the son of Acces a Thecaite, Abiezer an Anathothite,

text.NRSV: Sibbecai the Hushathite, Ilai the Ahohite,
text.Rotherham: Sibbecai, the Hushathite, Ilai, the Ahohite;
text.KJV: 11:29 Sibbecai the Hushathite, Ilai the Ahohite,
text.DRV: 11:29 Sobbochai an Husathite, Ilai an Ahohite,

text.NRSV: Maharai of Netophah, Heled son of Baanah of Netophah,
text.Rotherham: Maharai, the Netophathite, Heled, son of Baanah, the Netophathite;
text.KJV: 11:30 Maharai the Netophathite, Heled the sonne of Baanah the Netophathite,
text.DRV: 11:30 Maharai a Netophathite, Heled the son of Baana a Netophathite.

text.NRSV: Ithai son of Ribai of Gibeah of the Benjaminites, Benaiah of Pirathon,
text.Rotherham: Ithai, son of Ribai, of Gibeah, of the sons of Benjamin,—Benaiah, the Pirathonite;
text.KJV: 11:31 Ithai the sonne of Ribai of Gibeah, that perteined to the children of Beniamin, Benaiah the Pirathonite,
text.DRV: 11:31 Ethai the son of Ribai of Gabaath the children of Benjamin, Banaia a Pharatonite,

text.NRSV: Hurai of the wadis of Gaash, Abiel the Arbathite,
text.Rotherham: Hurai, of the torrents of Gaash, Abiel the Arbathite;
text.KJV: 11:32 Hurai of the brookes of Gaash, Abiel the Arbathite,
text.DRV: 11:32 Hurai of the Torrent Gaas, Abiel an Arbathite, Azmoth a Bauramite, Eliaba a Salabonite.

text.NRSV: Azmaveth of Bahurim, Eliahba of Shaalbon,
text.Rotherham: Azmaveth, the Baharumite, Eliahba, the Shaalbonite;
text.KJV: 11:33 Azmaueth the Baharumite, Elihaba the Shaalbonite,
text.DRV: 11:33 The sons of Assem a Gezonite, Jonathan the son of Sage an Ararite,

text.NRSV: Hashem the Gizonite, Jonathan son of Shagee the Hararite,
text.Rotherham: the sons of Hashem, the Gizonite, Jonathan, son of Shageh, the Hararite;
text.KJV: 11:34 The sonnes of Hashem the Gizonite: Ionathan the sonne of Shageh the Hararite,
text.DRV: 11:34 Ahiam the son of Sachar an Ararite,

text.NRSV: Ahiam son of Sachar the Hararite, Eliphal son of Ur,
text.Rotherham: Ahiam, son of Sacar, the Hararite, Eliphal, son of Ur;
text.KJV: 11:35 Ahiham the sonne of Sacar the Hararite, Eliphal the sonne of Ur,
text.DRV: 11:35 Eliphal the son of Ur,

text.NRSV: Hepher the Mecherathite, Ahijah the Pelonite,
text.Rotherham: Hepher, the Mecherathite, Ahijah the Pelonite;
text.KJV: 11:36 Hepher the Mecherathite, Ahiiah the Pelonite,
text.DRV: 11:36 Hepher a Mecherathite, Ahia Phelonite,

text.NRSV: Hezro of Carmel, Naarai son of Ezbai,
text.Rotherham: Hezro, the Carmelite, Naarai, son of Ezbai;
text.KJV: 11:37 Hezro the Carmelite, Naarai the sonne of Ezbai,
text.DRV: 11:37 Hesro a Carmelite, Naarai the son of Azbai,

text.NRSV: Joel the brother of Nathan, Mibhar son of Hagri,
text.Rotherham: Joel, brother of Nathan, Mibhar son of Hagri;
text.KJV: 11:38 Ioel the brother of Nathan, Mibhar the sonne of Haggeri,
text.DRV: 11:38 Joel the brother of Nathan, Mibahar the son of Agarai.

text.NRSV: Zelek the Ammonite, Naharai of Beeroth, the armor-bearer of Joab son of Zeruiah,
text.Rotherham: Zelek, the Ammonite,—Naharai, the Berothite, the armour-bearer of Joab son of Zeruiah;
text.KJV: 11:39 Zelek the Ammonite, Naharai the Berothite, the armour bearer of Ioab the sonne of Zeruiah,
text.DRV: 11:39 Selec an Ammonite, Naharai a Berothite the armour-bearer of Joab the son of Sarvia,

text.NRSV: Ira the Ithrite, Gareb the Ithrite,
text.Rotherham: Ira, the Ithrite, Gareb, the Ithrite;
text.KJV: 11:40 Ira the Ithrite, Gareb the Ithrite,
text.DRV: 11:40 Ira a Jethreite, Gareb a Jethreite,

text.NRSV: Uriah the Hittite, Zabad son of Ahlai,
text.Rotherham: Uriah, the Hittite, Zabad son of Ahlai;
text.KJV: 11:41 Uriah the Hittite, Zabad the sonne of Ahlai,
text.DRV: 11:41 Urias the Hetheite, Zabad the son of Oholi,

text.NRSV: Adina son of Shiza the Reubenite, a leader of the Reubenites, and thirty with him,
text.Rotherham: Adina, son of Shiza, the Reubenite, a chief of the Reubenites and over the thirty;
text.KJV: 11:42 Adina the sonne of Shiza the Reubenite, a captaine of the Reubenites, and thirtie with him,
text.DRV: 11:42 Adina the son of Siza a Rubenite the Prince of the Rubenites, and with him thirty:

text.NRSV: Hanan son of Maacah, and Joshaphat the Mithnite,
text.Rotherham: Hanan, son of Maacah, and Joshaphat, the Mithnite;
text.KJV: 11:43 Hanan the sonne of Maacah, and Ioshaphat the Mithnite,
text.DRV: 11:43 Hanan the son of Maacha, and Josaphat a Mathanite,

text.NRSV: Uzzia the Ashterathite, Shama and Jeiel sons of Hotham the Aroerite,
text.Rotherham: Uzzia, the Ashterathite,—Shama, and Jeiel, sons of Hotham, the Aroerite;
text.KJV: 11:44 Uzzia the Ashterathite, Shama and Iehiel the sonnes of Hothan the Aroerite,
text.DRV: 11:44 Ozia an Astarothite, Samma, and Jehiel the sons of Hotham an Arorite,

text.NRSV: Jediael son of Shimri, and his brother Joha the Tizite,
text.Rotherham: Jediael, son of Shimri, and Joha, his brother, the Tizite;
text.KJV: 11:45 Iediael the sonne of Zimri, and Ioha his brother, the Tizite,
text.DRV: 11:45 Jedihel the son of Zamri, and Joha his brother a Thosaite,

text.NRSV: Eliel the Mahavite, and Jeribai and Joshaviah sons of Elnaam, and Ithmah the Moabite,
text.Rotherham: Eliel, the Mahavite, and Jeribai and Joshaviah, sons of Elnaam,—and Ithmah the Moabite;
text.KJV: 11:46 Eliel the Mahauite, and Ieribai, and Ioshauiah the sonnes of Elnaan, and Ithmah the Moabite,
text.DRV: 11:46 Eliel a Mahumite, and Jeribai, and Josaia the sons of Elnaim, and Jethma a Moabite, Eliel, and Obed, and Jasiel of Masobia.

text.NRSV: Eliel, and Obed, and Jaasiel the Mezobaite.
text.Rotherham: Eliel, and Obed, and Jaasiel, of Zobah.
text.KJV: 11:47 Eliel, and Obed, and Iasiel the Mesobaite.

text.NRSV: The following are those who came to David at Ziklag, while he could not move about freely because of Saul son of Kish; they were among the warriors who helped him in war.
text.Rotherham: Now these, are they who came unto David, to Ziklag, he yet being shut up, because of Saul son of Kish,—and, they, were among the heroes, helpers in the war;
text.KJV: 12:1 Now these are they that came to Dauid to Ziklag while hee yet kept himselfe close, because of Saul the sonne of Kish: and they were among the mighty men, helpers of the warre.
text.DRV: 12:1 These also came to David into Siceleg, when as yet he fled from Saul the son of Cis, the which were most valiant and excellent warriors,

text.NRSV: They were archers who could shoot arrows and sling stones with either the right hand or the left; they were Benjaminites, Saul’s kindred.
text.Rotherham: armed with the bow, using right hand or left with stones, and with arrows in the bow,—of the brethren of Saul, out of Benjamin:
text.KJV: 12:2 They were armed with bowes, and could vse both the right hand and the left, in hurling stones, and shooting arrowes out of a bow, euen of Sauls brethren of Beniamin.
text.DRV: 12:2 drawing the bow, & hurling with both hands stones in slings, & shooting arrows directly: of the brethren of Saul of Benjamin.

text.NRSV: The chief was Ahiezer, then Joash, both sons of Shemaah of Gibeah; also Jeziel and Pelet sons of Azmaveth; Beracah, Jehu of Anathoth,
text.Rotherham: the chief, Ahiezer, and Joash, sons of Shemaah the Gibeathite, and Jeziel and Pelet, sons of Azmaveth,—and Beracah, and Jehu the Anathothite;
text.KJV: 12:3 The chiefe was Ahiezer, then Ioash the sonnes of Shemaah the Gibeathite, and Ieziel, and Pelet, the sonnes of Azmaueth, and Berachah, and Iehu the Antothite,
text.DRV: 12:3 The Prince Ahiezer, and Joas the sons of Samaa a Gabaathite, and Jaziel, and Phallet the sons of Azmoth, and Beracha, and Jehu an Anothothite.

text.NRSV: Ishmaiah of Gibeon, a warrior among the Thirty and a leader over the Thirty; Jeremiah, Jahaziel, Johanan, Jozabad of Gederah,
text.Rotherham: and Ishmaiah, the Gibeonite, a hero among the thirty, and over the thirty,—and Jeremiah and Jahaziel, and Johanan, and Jozabad, the Gederathite;
text.KJV: 12:4 And Ismaiah the Gibeonite, a mightie man among the thirtie, and ouer the thirtie, and Ieremiah, and Iahaziel, and Iohanan, and Iosabad the Gederathite,
text.DRV: 12:4 Samaias also a Gabaonite the most valiant amongst the thirty and above the thirty. Jeremias, and Jeheziel, and Johanan, & Jezabad a Gaderothite.

text.NRSV: Eluzai, Jerimoth, Bealiah, Shemariah, Shephatiah the Haruphite;
text.Rotherham: Eluzai and Jerimoth and Bealiah and Shemariah, and Shephatiah, the Haruphite;
text.KJV: 12:5 Eleuzai, and Ierimoth, and Bealiath, and Shemariah, and Shephatiah the Haruphite,
text.DRV: 12:5 And Eluzai, and Jerimuth, and Baalia, & Samaria, and Saphatia an Haruphite.

text.NRSV: Elkanah, Isshiah, Azarel, Joezer, and Jashobeam, the Korahites;
text.Rotherham: Elkanah and Isshiah, and Azarel, and Joezer and Jashobeam, the Korahites;
text.KJV: 12:6 Elkanah, and Iesiah, and Azariel, and Ioezer, and Iashobeam, the Korhites,
text.DRV: 12:6 Elcana, and Jesia, and Azareel, and Joezer, and Jesbaam of Carehim:

text.NRSV: and Joelah and Zebadiah, sons of Jeroham of Gedor.
text.Rotherham: and Joelah and Zebadiah, sons of Jeroham, of Gedor.
text.KJV: 12:7 And Ioelah, and Zebadiah the sonnes of Ieroam of Gedor.
text.DRV: 12:7 Joela also, and Zabadia the sons of Jeroham of Gedor.

text.NRSV: From the Gadites there went over to David at the stronghold in the wilderness mighty and experienced warriors, expert with shield and spear, whose faces were like the faces of lions, and who were swift as gazelles on the mountains:
text.Rotherham: And, of the Gadites, there separated themselves unto David, to the stronghold towards the desert, heroes of valour, men of war, for battle, men that could handle shield and spear,—and, faces of lions, were their faces, and, like gazelles upon the mountains, were they, for swiftness:
text.KJV: 12:8 And of the Gadites there separated themselues vnto Dauid, into the hold to the wildernesse, men of might, and men of warre, fit for the battel, that could handle shield and buckler, whose faces were like the faces of Lyons, and were as swift as the Roes vpon the mountaines:
text.DRV: 12:8 Yea and of Gaddi also there fled to David, when he lay hid in the desert most valiant men, & the best warriors, holding shield and spear: their faces as the faces of a lion, & swift as the roe-bucks on the mountains:

text.NRSV: Ezer the chief, Obadiah second, Eliab third,
text.Rotherham: Ezer, the chief, Obadiah, the second,—Eliab, the third;
text.KJV: 12:9 Ezer the first, Obadiah the second, Eliab the third,
text.DRV: 12:9 Ezer the Prince, Obdias the second, Eliab the third,

text.NRSV: Mishmannah fourth, Jeremiah fifth,
text.Rotherham: Mashmannah, the fourth, Jeremiah, the fifth;
text.KJV: 12:10 Mashmannah the fourth, Ieremiah the fift,
text.DRV: 12:10 Masmana the fourth, Jeremias the fifth,

text.NRSV: Attai sixth, Eliel seventh,
text.Rotherham: Attai, the sixth, Eliel the seventh;
text.KJV: 12:11 Atthai the sixt, Eliel the seuenth,
text.DRV: 12:11 Ethi the sixth, Eliel the seventh,

text.NRSV: Johanan eighth, Elzabad ninth,
text.Rotherham: Johanan, the eighth, Elzabad, the ninth;
text.KJV: 12:12 Iohanan the eighth, Elzabad the ninth,
text.DRV: 12:12 Johanan the eight, Elzebad the ninth,

text.NRSV: Jeremiah tenth, Machbannai eleventh.
text.Rotherham: Jeremiah, the tenth, Machbannai, the eleventh.
text.KJV: 12:13 Ieremiah the tenth, Machbanai the eleuenth.
text.DRV: 12:13 Jeremias the tenth, Machbanai the eleventh,

text.NRSV: These Gadites were officers of the army, the least equal to a hundred and the greatest to a thousand.
text.Rotherham: These, were of the sons of Gad, chiefs of the host,—one to a hundred, the least, and, the greatest, to a thousand.
text.KJV: 12:14 These were of the sonnes of Gad, captaines of the hoste: one of the least was ouer an hundred, and the greatest, ouer a thousand.
text.DRV: 12:14 these of the children of Gad were the Princes of his army. The meanest was captain over an hundred soldiers, & the greatest, over a thousand.

text.NRSV: These are the men who crossed the Jordan in the first month, when it was overflowing all its banks, and put to flight all those in the valleys, to the east and to the west.
text.Rotherham: These, are they who passed over the Jordan, in the first month, when, it, was full, over all its banks,—and they put to flight all them of the vales, to the east and to the west.
text.KJV: 12:15 These are they that went ouer Iorden in the first moneth, when it had ouerflowen all his bankes, and they put to flight all them of the valleis, both toward the East, and toward the West.
text.DRV: 12:15 These are they which passed Jordan the first month, when it used to flow over his banks: & they chased away all that dwelt in the valleys toward the east quarter and the west.

text.NRSV: Some Benjaminites and Judeans came to the stronghold to David.
text.Rotherham: And there came, of the sons of Benjamin, and Judah, as far as the stronghold, unto David.
text.KJV: 12:16 And there came of the children of Beniamin, and Iudah, to the hold vnto Dauid.
text.DRV: 12:16 And there came also of Benjamin, & of Juda to the hold, wherein David abode.

text.NRSV: David went out to meet them and said to them, “If you have come to me in friendship to help me, then my heart will be knit to you; but if you have come to betray me to my adversaries, though my hands have done no wrong, then may the God of our ancestors see and give judgment.”
text.Rotherham: And David went forth to meet them, and responded, and said to them, If, peaceably, ye are come unto me, to help me, I shall have towards you, a heart for unity,—but, if to betray me to mine adversaries, there being no violence in my hands, may the God of our fathers see and rebuke!
text.KJV: 12:17 And Dauid went out to meete them, and answered and sayd vnto them: If yee bee come peaceably vnto me to helpe me, mine heart shall be knit vnto you: but if yee be come to betray me to mine enemies, seeing there is no wrong in mine hands: the God of our fathers looke thereon, and rebuke it.
text.DRV: 12:17 And David went out to meet them, & said: If you be come peaceably to me for to help me, my heart be joined to you, but if you lie in wait against me for my adversaries, whereas I have no iniquity in my hands, the God of our fathers see, and judge.

text.NRSV: Then the spirit came upon Amasai, chief of the Thirty, and he said, “We are yours, O David; and with you, O son of Jesse! Peace, peace to you, and peace to the one who helps you! For your God is the one who helps you.” Then David received them, and made them officers of his troops.
text.Rotherham: Then, the spirit, clothed Amasai, chief of the thirty, Thine, O David, Yea, with, thee, O son of Jesse! Prosperity, prosperity to thee, And prosperity to thy helpers, For thy God, hath helped thee: So David accepted them, and set them among the chiefs of the band.
text.KJV: 12:18 Then the spirit came vpon Amasai, who was chiefe of the captaines, and he sayd, Thine are we, Dauid, and on thy side, thou sonne of Iesse: Peace, peace be vnto thee, and peace be to thine helpers; for thy God helpeth thee. Then Dauid receiued them, and made them captaines of the band.
text.DRV: 12:18 But the spirit came on Amasai the Prince among thirty, & he said: We are thine, o David, & with thee, o son of Isai: peace, peace be to thee, and peace to thy helpers, for thy God helpeth thee. David therefore received them, and appointed them Princes of the band.

text.NRSV: Some Manassites deserted to David when he came with the Philistines for the battle against Saul. (Yet he did not help them, for the lords of the Philistines took counsel and sent him away, saying, “He will desert to his master Saul at the cost of our heads.”)
text.Rotherham: And, of Manasseh, there fell away unto David, when he came with the Philistines against Saul to battle, but they helped them not,—for the lords of the Philistines advisedly sent him away, saying, At the price of our heads, he might fall away to his master, Saul.
text.KJV: 12:19 And there fell some of Manasseh to Dauid, when he came with the Philistines against Saul to battell, but they helped them not. For the Lords of the Philistines, vpon aduisement, sent him away, saying, Hee will fall to his master Saul, to the ieopardie of our heads.
text.DRV: 12:19 Moreover of Manasses there fled to David, when he came with the Philisthiims against Saul to fight, and he fought not with them: because the Princes of the Philisthiims taking counsel sent him back, saying: with the peril of our head will he return to his lord Saul.

text.NRSV: As he went to Ziklag these Manassites deserted to him: Adnah, Jozabad, Jediael, Michael, Jozabad, Elihu, and Zillethai, chiefs of thousands in Manasseh.
text.Rotherham: When he went into Ziklag, there fell away unto him, of Manasseh, Adnah and Jozabad, and Jediael and Michael and Jozabad, and Elihu and Zillethai,—chiefs of the thousands that pertained to Manasseh;
text.KJV: 12:20 As he went to Ziklag, there fell to him of Manasseh, Adnah, and Iozabad, and Iediel, and Michael, and Iozabad, and Elihu, and Zilthai, captaines of the thousands that were of Manasseh.
text.DRV: 12:20 Therefore when he returned into Siceleg, there fled to him of Manasses, Ednas, and Jozabab, and Jedihel, and Michael, and Ednas, & Jozabad, and Eliu, and Salathi, the Princes of a thousand in Manasses.

text.NRSV: They helped David against the band of raiders, for they were all mighty warriors and were commanders in the army.
text.Rotherham: and, they, helped with David, against the [marauding] band, for, heroes of valour, were they all,—and they became captains in the host;
text.KJV: 12:21 And they helped Dauid against the band of the Rouers: for they were all mighty men of valour, and were captaines in the hoste.
text.DRV: 12:21 These did aid David against the rovers: for they were all most valiant men, & were made commanders in the army.

text.NRSV: Indeed from day to day people kept coming to David to help him, until there was a great army, like an army of God.
text.Rotherham: for, from day to day, there came unto David, to help him,—until it was a great camp, like a camp of God.
text.KJV: 12:22 For at that time day by day, there came to Dauid to helpe him, vntill it was a great hoste, like the hoste of God.
text.DRV: 12:22 Yea & there came every day to David to help him, till it became a great number, as it were the army of God.

text.NRSV: These are the numbers of the divisions of the armed troops who came to David in Hebron to turn the kingdom of Saul over to him, according to the word of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the numbers of the chiefs armed for war, they came unto David, to Hebron,—to turn round the kingdom of Saul unto him, according to the bidding of Yahweh:
text.KJV: 12:23 ¶ And these are the numbers of the bands, that were ready armed to the warre, and came to Dauid to Hebron, to turne the kingdome of Saul to him, according to the word of the Lord.
text.DRV: 12:23 This also is the number of the commanders of the army, which came to David, when he was in Hebron, to transfer the kingdom of Saul to him, according to the word of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Of the Judeans carrying shield and spear, six thousand eight hundred armed troops.
text.Rotherham: Sons of Judah, bearers of shield and spear, six thousand and eight hundred, armed for war.
text.KJV: 12:24 The children of Iudah that bare shield, and speare, were sixe thousand, and eight hundred, readie armed to the warre.
text.DRV: 12:24 The children of Juda bearing shield and spear, six thousand eight hundred well appointed to battle.

text.NRSV: Of the Simeonites, mighty warriors, seven thousand one hundred.
text.Rotherham: Of sons of Simeon, heroes of valour for the war, seven thousand, and one hundred.
text.KJV: 12:25 Of the children of Simeon, mighty men of valour for the warre, seuen thousand and one hundred.
text.DRV: 12:25 Of the children of Simeon valiant men to fight, seven thousand one hundred.

text.NRSV: Of the Levites, four thousand six hundred.
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Levi, four thousand, and six hundred.
text.KJV: 12:26 Of the children of Leui, foure thousand and sixe hundred.
text.DRV: 12:26 Of the children of Levi, four thousand six hundred.

text.NRSV: Jehoiada, leader of the house of Aaron, and with him three thousand seven hundred.
text.Rotherham: And Jehoiada, was chief ruler for Aaron,—and, with him, three thousand and seven hundred;
text.KJV: 12:27 And Iehoiada was the leader of the Aaronits, and with him were three thousand, and seuen hundred.
text.DRV: 12:27 Joiada also Prince of the stock of Aaron, and with him three thousand seven hundred.

text.NRSV: Zadok, a young warrior, and twenty-two commanders from his own ancestral house.
text.Rotherham: and Zadok, a young man, a hero of valour,—and the house of his father, captains twenty and two.
text.KJV: 12:28 And Zadok a young man mightie of valour, and of his fathers house twentie and two captaines.
text.DRV: 12:28 Sadoc also a young man of goodly towardness, and the house of his father, Princes twenty two.

text.NRSV: Of the Benjaminites, the kindred of Saul, three thousand, of whom the majority had continued to keep their allegiance to the house of Saul.
text.Rotherham: And, of the sons of Benjamin, brethren of Saul, three thousand,—and, hitherto, the greater part of them, had been keeping the charge of the house of Saul.
text.KJV: 12:29 And of the children of Beniamin the kinred of Saul three thousand: for hitherto the greatest part of them had kept the ward of the house of Saul.
text.DRV: 12:29 And of the children of Benjamin the brethren of Saul, three thousand: for a great part of them as yet followed the house of Saul.

text.NRSV: Of the Ephraimites, twenty thousand eight hundred, mighty warriors, famous in their ancestral houses.
text.Rotherham: And, of the sons of Ephraim, twenty thousand, and eight hundred,—heroes of valour, men of renown, to their ancestral house.
text.KJV: 12:30 And of the children of Ephraim, twentie thousand, and eight hundred, mightie men of valour, famous throughout the house of their fathers.
text.DRV: 12:30 Moreover of the children of Ephraim twenty thousand eight hundred valiant of strength, men renowned in their kindreds.

text.NRSV: Of the half-tribe of Manasseh, eighteen thousand, who were expressly named to come and make David king.
text.Rotherham: And, of the half tribe of Manasseh, eighteen thousand, who were distinguished by name, coming in to make David king.
text.KJV: 12:31 And of the halfe tribe of Manasseh, eighteene thousand, which were expressed by name, to come and make Dauid king.
text.DRV: 12:31 And of the half tribe of Manasses, eighteen thousand, every one by their names, came to make David king.

text.NRSV: Of Issachar, those who had understanding of the times, to know what Israel ought to do, two hundred chiefs, and all their kindred under their command.
text.Rotherham: And, of the sons of Issachar, such as were of good understanding of the times, to know what Israel, should do, their chiefs, were two hundred, and, all their brethren, were at their bidding.
text.KJV: 12:32 And of the children of Issachar, which were men that had vnderstanding of the times, to know what Israel ought to doe: the heads of them were two hundred, and all their brethren were at their commandement.
text.DRV: 12:32 Also of the children of Issachar men of understanding, that knew all times to command what Israel should do, Princes two hundred: and all the rest of the tribe did follow their counsel.

text.NRSV: Of Zebulun, fifty thousand seasoned troops, equipped for battle with all the weapons of war, to help David with singleness of purpose.
text.Rotherham: Of Zebulun—such as were ready to go forth in host, expert for battle with all weapons of war, fifty thousand,—and for setting in array, not of two minds!
text.KJV: 12:33 Of Zebulun, such as went foorth to battell, expert in warre, with all instruments of warre, fifty thousand, which could keepe ranke: They were not of double heart.
text.DRV: 12:33 Moreover of Zabulon such as went forth to battle, and stood in array well appointed with armour of war, there came fifty thousand to aid, not in a double heart.

text.NRSV: Of Naphtali, a thousand commanders, with whom there were thirty-seven thousand armed with shield and spear.
text.Rotherham: And, of Naphtali, a thousand captains,—and, with them, with shield and spear, thirty-seven thousand.
text.KJV: 12:34 And of Naphtali a thousand captaines, and with them, with shield and speare, thirtie and seuen thousand.
text.DRV: 12:34 And of Nephthali, commanders a thousand: & with them furnished with shield & spear, seven & thirty thousand.

text.NRSV: Of the Danites, twenty-eight thousand six hundred equipped for battle.
text.Rotherham: And, of the Danite, expert for war, twenty-eight thousand, and six hundred.
text.KJV: 12:35 And of the Danites, expert in war, twentie and eight thousand, and sixe hundred.
text.DRV: 12:35 Of Dan also prepared to battle, twenty eight thousand six hundred.

text.NRSV: Of Asher, forty thousand seasoned troops ready for battle.
text.Rotherham: And, of Asher, such as were ready to go forth in host to set in array for battle, forty thousand.
text.KJV: 12:36 And of Asher, such as went foorth to battell, expert in warre, fourtie thousand.
text.DRV: 12:36 And of Aser going forth to fight, & provoking in battle, forty thousand.

text.NRSV: Of the Reubenites and Gadites and the half-tribe of Manasseh from beyond the Jordan, one hundred twenty thousand armed with all the weapons of war.
text.Rotherham: And, from over the Jordan, of the Reubenites and Gadites and half tribe of Manasseh, with all manner of weapons of war for battle, a hundred and twenty thousand.
text.KJV: 12:37 And on the other side of Iorden, of the Reubenites, & the Gadites, and of the halfe tribe of Manasseh, with all maner of instruments of warre for the battell, an hundred and twentie thousand.
text.DRV: 12:37 And beyond Jordan of the children of Ruben & of Gad, & the half part of the tribe of Manasses, furnished with armour of war, an hundred twenty thousand.

text.NRSV: All these, warriors arrayed in battle order, came to Hebron with full intent to make David king over all Israel; likewise all the rest of Israel were of a single mind to make David king.
text.Rotherham: All these, being men of war, expert in setting in battle-array, with a perfect heart, came to Hebron, to make David king over all Israel,—moreover also, all the rest of Israel, were of one heart to make David king.
text.KJV: 12:38 All these men of warre, that could keepe ranke, came with a perfect heart to Hebron, to make Dauid king ouer all Israel: and all the rest also of Israel, were of one heart to make Dauid king.
text.DRV: 12:38 All these men of war well appointed to fight, with perfect heart came into Hebron, to make David king over all Israel: yea & all the rest of Israel were of one heart, that David should be made king.

text.NRSV: They were there with David for three days, eating and drinking, for their kindred had provided for them.
text.Rotherham: So they were there, with David, three days, eating and drinking,—for their brethren had made preparation for them.
text.KJV: 12:39 And there they were with Dauid three dayes, eating and drinking: for their brethren had prepared for them.
text.DRV: 12:39 And they were there with David three days eating and drinking: for their brethren had prepared for them.

text.NRSV: And also their neighbors, from as far as Issachar and Zebulun and Naphtali, were bringing food on donkeys, camels, mules, and oxen—abundant provisions of meal, cakes of figs, clusters of raisins, wine, oil, oxen, and sheep, for there was joy in Israel.
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, they who were near of kin unto them, as far as Issachar and Zebulun and Naphtali, were bringing bread, on asses and on camels and on mules and on oxen, meal-food, cakes of figs and cakes of raisins, and wine, and oil, and oxen, and sheep—in abundance,—for there was joy Israel.
text.KJV: 12:40 Moreouer, they that were nigh them, euen vnto Issachar, and Zebulun, and Naphtali brought bread on asses, and on camels, and on mules, and on oxen, and meat, meale, cakes of figs, and bunches of raisins, and wine, and oyle, and oxen, and sheepe abundantly: for there was ioy in Israel.
text.DRV: 12:40 Yea and they that were near them as far as Issachar, and Zabulon, and Nephthali, brought loaves on Asses, and on camels, and on mules, and upon oxen, to eat: meal, figs, raisins, wine, oil, beeves, muttons, in all aboundance, for there was joy in Israel.

text.NRSV: David consulted with the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, with every leader.
text.Rotherham: And David consulted with the rulers of thousands and hundreds, even with every chief ruler.
text.KJV: 13:1 And Dauid consulted with the captaines of thousands, and hundreds, and with euery leader.
text.DRV: 13:1 And David took counsel with the tribunes, and centurions, and all the commanders,

text.NRSV: David said to the whole assembly of Israel, “If it seems good to you, and if it is the will of the Lord our God, let us send abroad to our kindred who remain in all the territories of Israel, and with them to the priests and Levites in the towns that have pasture lands, that they may come together to us.
text.Rotherham: And David said to all the convocation of Israel, If, unto you, it seemeth to be good, and from Yahweh our God, we will urgently send unto our brethren who remain in all the lands of Israel, and, with them, the priests and the Levites in their pasture-land cities,—that they may gather together unto us;
text.KJV: 13:2 And Dauid said vnto all the Congregation of Israel, If it seeme good vnto you, and that it be of the Lord our God, let vs send abroad vnto our brethren euery where, that are left in all the land of Israel, and with them also to the Priests and Leuites which are in their cities and suburbs, that they may gather themselues vnto vs.
text.DRV: 13:2 and he said to all the assembly of Israel: If it please you, & if the word which I speak proceed from our Lord God, let us send to the rest of our brethren into all the countries of Israel, & to the Priests, & Levites, that dwell in the suburbs of the cities, that they may be gathered together unto us,

text.NRSV: Then let us bring again the ark of our God to us; for we did not seek it in the days of Saul.”
text.Rotherham: and let us bring round the ark of our God, unto us,—For they sought it not, in the days of Saul.
text.KJV: 13:3 And let vs bring againe the Arke of our God to vs: for wee enquired not at it in the dayes of Saul.
text.DRV: 13:3 and we may bring again unto us the Ark of our God: for we sought it not in the days of Saul.

text.NRSV: The whole assembly agreed to do so, for the thing scarcely seemed right in the eyes of all the people.
text.Rotherham: Than said all the convocation, that it should be done thus,—for right was the thing, in the eyes of all the people.
text.KJV: 13:4 And all the Congregation saide, that they would doe so: for the thing was right in the eyes of all the people.
text.DRV: 13:4 And the whole multitude answered that it should be so done: for the word had pleased all the people.

text.NRSV: So David assembled all Israel from the Shihor of Egypt to Lebo-hamath, to bring the ark of God from Kiriath-jearim.
text.Rotherham: So David convoked all Israel, from Shihor of Egypt, even unto the entering in of Hamath,—to bring the ark of God from Kiriath-jearim.
text.KJV: 13:5 So Dauid gathered all Israel together, from Shihor of Egypt, euen vnto the entring of Hemath, to bring the Arke of God from Kiriath-iearim.
text.DRV: 13:5 David therefore assembled all Israel from Sihor of Ægypt, till thou enter into Emath, to bring the Ark of God from Cariathiarim.

text.NRSV: And David and all Israel went up to Baalah, that is, to Kiriath-jearim, which belongs to Judah, to bring up from there the ark of God, which is called by the name of the Lord who is enthroned on the cherubim.
text.Rotherham: And David went up and all Israel to Baalah, unto Kiriath-jearim, which pertaineth to Judah,—to bring up from thence, the ark of God—Yahweh who inhabiteth the cherubim, on which is called the Name.
text.KJV: 13:6 And Dauid went vp, and all Israel, to Baalah, that is, to Kiriath-iearim, which belonged to Iudah, to bring vp thence the Arke of God the Lord, that dwelleth betweene the Cherubims, whose name is called on it.
text.DRV: 13:6 And David went up, and every man of Israel to the hill of Cariathiarim which is in Juda, to fetch thence the Ark of our God sitting upon the Cherubim, where his name is invocated.

text.NRSV: They carried the ark of God on a new cart, from the house of Abinadab, and Uzzah and Ahio were driving the cart.
text.Rotherham: And they carried the ark of God, upon a new waggon, out of the house of Aminadab,—and, Uzza and Ahio, were driving the waggon.
text.KJV: 13:7 And they caried the Arke of God in a new cart, out of the house of Abinadab: and Uzza, and Ahio draue the cart.
text.DRV: 13:7 And they laid the Ark of God upon a new wain, out of the house of Abinadab. And Oza and his brother did drive the wain.

text.NRSV: David and all Israel were dancing before God with all their might, with song and lyres and harps and tambourines and cymbals and trumpets.
text.Rotherham: And, David and all Israel, were dancing for joy before God, with all boldness, and with songs and with lyres, and with harps and with timbrels, and with cymbals, and with trumpets.
text.KJV: 13:8 And Dauid and all Israel played before God with all their might, and with singing, and with harpes, and with psalteries, and with tymbrels, and with cymbals, and with trumpets.
text.DRV: 13:8 Moreover David, & all Israel played before our Lord with all their might in songs, and on harps, and psalteries, and timbrels, and cymbals, and trumpets.

text.NRSV: When they came to the threshing floor of Chidon, Uzzah put out his hand to hold the ark, for the oxen shook it.
text.Rotherham: And, when they came as far as the threshing-floor of Chidon, Uzza thrust forth his hand, to seize the ark, for the oxen were restive.
text.KJV: 13:9 ¶ And when they came vnto the threshing floore of Chidon, Uzza put foorth his hand to hold the Arke, for the oxen stumbled.
text.DRV: 13:9 And when they were come to the Floor of Chidon, Oza stretched forth his hand, to hold up the Ark: for the oxe being wanton had made it lean a little a-side.

text.NRSV: The anger of the Lord was kindled against Uzzah; he struck him down because he put out his hand to the ark; and he died there before God.
text.Rotherham: Then was kindled the anger of Yahweh against Uzza, and he smote him, because he thrust forth his hand unto the ark,—and he died there, before God.
text.KJV: 13:10 And the anger of the Lord was kindled against Uzza, and hee smote him, because hee put his hand to the Arke: and there he died before God.
text.DRV: 13:10 Our Lord therefore was angry against Oza, & struck him, for that he had touched the Ark: and he died there before our Lord.

text.NRSV: David was angry because the Lord had burst out against Uzzah; so that place is called Perez-uzzah to this day.
text.Rotherham: And it was a sadness unto David, that Yahweh had broken in with a breach against Uzza,—and he called that place Perez-uzza, [as it is called] unto this day.
text.KJV: 13:11 And Dauid was displeased, because the Lord had made a breach vpon Uzza; wherefore that place is called Perez-Uzza, to this day.
text.DRV: 13:11 And David was strucken sad, because our Lord had divided Oza: and he called that place, the Division of Oza until this present day.

text.NRSV: David was afraid of God that day; he said, “How can I bring the ark of God home to me?”
text.Rotherham: And David was afraid of God, on that day, saying,—How can I bring unto me, the ark of God?
text.KJV: 13:12 And Dauid was afraide of God that day, saying, How shall I bring the Arke of God home to me?
