text.DRV: 13:12 And he feared God at that time, saying: How may I bring in the Ark of God unto me?

text.NRSV: So David did not take the ark into the city of David; he delivered it into the house of Obed-edom the Gittite.
text.Rotherham: So David removed not the ark unto him, into the city of David,—but took it aside into the house of Obed-edom, the Gittite.
text.KJV: 13:13 So Dauid brought not the Arke home to himselfe to the city of Dauid, but caried it aside into the house of Obed-Edom the Gittite.
text.DRV: 13:13 And for this cause he brought it not unto himself, that is, into the city of David, but turned it away into the house of Obededom the Getheite.

text.NRSV: The ark of God remained with the family of Obed-edom in his house three months; and the Lord blessed the house of Obed-edom and all that he had.
text.Rotherham: And the ark of God remained with the household of Obed-edom, in his house, three months,—and Yahweh blessed the household of Obed-edom, and all that he had.
text.KJV: 13:14 And the Arke of God remained with the family of Obed-Edom in his house three moneths. And the Lord blessed the house of Obed-Edom, and all that he had.
text.DRV: 13:14 Therefore the Ark of God remained in the house of Obededom three months: & our Lord blessed his house, and all things that he had.

text.NRSV: King Hiram of Tyre sent messengers to David, along with cedar logs, and masons and carpenters to build a house for him.
text.Rotherham: And Hiram king of Tyre sent messengers unto David, and timber of cedars, with masons and artificers, to build for him a house.
text.KJV: 14:1 Now Hiram king of Tyre sent messengers to Dauid, and timber of Cedars, with masons, and carpenters to build him an house.
text.DRV: 14:1 Hiram also the king of Tyre sent messengers to David, and cedar trees, and artificers for walls, and timber, to build him a house.

text.NRSV: David then perceived that the Lord had established him as king over Israel, and that his kingdom was highly exalted for the sake of his people Israel.
text.Rotherham: So David perceived, that Yahweh, had confirmed him, as king over Israel,—that his kingship was exalted, for the sake of his people Israel.
text.KJV: 14:2 And Dauid perceiued that the Lord had confirmed him king ouer Israel, for his kingdome was lift vp on high, because of his people Israel.
text.DRV: 14:2 And David knew that our Lord had established him to be king over Israel, and that his Kingdom was exalted over his people Israel.

text.NRSV: David took more wives in Jerusalem, and David became the father of more sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: And David took more wives, in Jerusalem,—and David begat more sons and daughters.
text.KJV: 14:3 ¶ And Dauid tooke moe wiues at Ierusalem: and Dauid begate moe sonnes and daughters.
text.DRV: 14:3 David also took other wives in Jerusalem: and he begat sons, and daughters.

text.NRSV: These are the names of the children who were born to him in Jerusalem: Shammua, Shobab, Nathan, Solomon,
text.Rotherham: Now, these, are the names of them who were born, whom he had in Jerusalem,—Shammua and Shobab, Nathan, and Solomon;
text.KJV: 14:4 Now these are the names of his children which hee had in Ierusalem: Shammua, and Shobab, Nathan, and Solomon,
text.DRV: 14:4 And these be their names, that were born to him in Jerusalem: Samua, and Sobab, Nathan, and Salomon.

text.NRSV: Ibhar, Elishua, Elpelet,
text.Rotherham: and Ibhar and Elishua, and Elpelet;
text.KJV: 14:5 And Ibhar, and Elishua, and Elpalet,
text.DRV: 14:5 Jebahar, and Elisua, and Eliphalet,

text.NRSV: Nogah, Nepheg, Japhia,
text.Rotherham: and Nogah and Nepheg, and Japhia;
text.KJV: 14:6 And Noga, and Nepheg, and Iaphia,
text.DRV: 14:6 Noga also, and Napheg, and Japhia,

text.NRSV: Elishama, Beeliada, and Eliphelet.
text.Rotherham: and Elishama and Beeliada, and Eliphelet.
text.KJV: 14:7 And Elishama, and Beeliada, and Elpalet.
text.DRV: 14:7 Elisama, and Baalaida, and Eliphalet.

text.NRSV: When the Philistines heard that David had been anointed king over all Israel, all the Philistines went up in search of David; and David heard of it and went out against them.
text.Rotherham: And, when the Philistines heard that David had been anointed king over all Israel, all the Philistines came up to seek to secure David,—and David, hearing, went out against them.
text.KJV: 14:8 ¶ And when the Philistines heard that Dauid was anoynted king ouer all Israel, all the Philistines went vp to seeke Dauid: and Dauid heard of it, and went out against them.
text.DRV: 14:8 And the Philisthiims hearing that David was anointed for king over all Israel, they went up all to seek him: which when David had heard, he went out to meet them.

text.NRSV: Now the Philistines had come and made a raid in the valley of Rephaim.
text.Rotherham: Now, the Philistines, had come and spread themselves out, in the vale of Rephaim.
text.KJV: 14:9 And the Philistines came & spread themselues in the valley of Rephaim.
text.DRV: 14:9 Moreover the Philisthiims coming, were spread abroad in the Vale Raphaim.

text.NRSV: David inquired of God, “Shall I go up against the Philistines? Will you give them into my hand?” The Lord said to him, “Go up, and I will give them into your hand.”
text.Rotherham: Then David asked of God, saying, Shall I go up against the Philistines? and wilt thou deliver them into my hand? And Yahweh said to him, Go up, and I will deliver them into thy hand.
text.KJV: 14:10 And Dauid enquired of God, saying, Shall I goe vp against the Philistines? and wilt thou deliuer them into mine hand? And the Lord said vnto him, Go vp, for I will deliuer them into thine hand.
text.DRV: 14:10 And David consulted our Lord, saying: Shall I go up to the Philistians, and wilt thou deliver them into my hand? And our Lord said to him: Go up, and I will deliver them into thy hand.

text.NRSV: So they went up to Baal-perazim, and David defeated them there. David said, “God has burst out against my enemies by my hand, like a bursting flood.” Therefore that place is called Baal-perazim.
text.Rotherham: So they came up in Baal-perazim, and David smote them there, and David said, God hath broken forth against mine enemies by my hand, like the breaking forth of waters,—For this cause, called they the name of that place, Baal-perazim.
text.KJV: 14:11 So they came vp to Baal-Perazim, and Dauid smote them there. Then Dauid said, God hath broken in vpon mine enemies by mine hand, like the breaking foorth of waters: therefore they called the name of that place, Baal-Perazim.
text.DRV: 14:11 And when they were come up into Baalpharasim, David struck them there, and said: God hath divided mine enemies by my hand, as waters are divided: and therefore the name of that place was called Baal Pharasim.

text.NRSV: They abandoned their gods there, and at David’s command they were burned.
text.Rotherham: And they left their gods there,—and David gave the word, and they were burned up in fire.
text.KJV: 14:12 And when they had left their gods there, Dauid gaue a commandement, and they were burnt with fire.
text.DRV: 14:12 And they left there their gods, which David commanded to be burnt.

text.NRSV: Once again the Philistines made a raid in the valley.
text.Rotherham: And the Philistines yet again spread themselves out in the vale.
text.KJV: 14:13 And the Philistines yet againe spread themselues abroad in the valley.
text.DRV: 14:13 And other time also the Philisthiims invaded, and were dispersed in the Vale.

text.NRSV: When David again inquired of God, God said to him, “You shall not go up after them; go around and come on them opposite the balsam trees.
text.Rotherham: So David, asked again, of God, and God said to him, Thou shalt not go up after them,—get thee round, away from them, so shalt thou come in upon them, over against the mulberry-trees;
text.KJV: 14:14 Therfore Dauid enquired againe of God, and God said vnto him, Goe not vp after them, turne away from them, and come vpon them ouer against the mulbery trees.
text.DRV: 14:14 And David consulted God again, and God said to him: Go not up after them, retire from them, & thou shalt come against them over against the pear-trees.

text.NRSV: For when you hear the sound of marching in the tops of the balsam trees, then go out to battle; for God has gone out before you to strike down the army of the Philistines.”
text.Rotherham: and it shall be, when thou hearest a sound of marching in the tops of the mulberry-trees, then, shalt thou go forth into the battle,—for God will have gone forth before thee, to smite the host of the Philistines.
text.KJV: 14:15 And it shall bee, when thou shalt heare a sound of going in the tops of the mulbery trees, that then thou shalt goe out to battaile: for God is gone foorth before thee, to smite the hoste of the Philistines.
text.DRV: 14:15 And when thou shalt hear the sound of one going in the top of the pear-trees, then shalt thou issue forth to battle. For God is gone forth before thee, to strike the camp of the Philisthiims.

text.NRSV: David did as God had commanded him, and they struck down the Philistine army from Gibeon to Gezer.
text.Rotherham: So David did as God commanded him,—and they smote the host of the Philistines, from Gibeon even unto Gezer.
text.KJV: 14:16 Dauid therefore did as God commanded him: and they smote the hoste of the Philistines from Gibeon euen to Gazer.
text.DRV: 14:16 David therefore did as God had commanded him, & struck the camp of the Philisthians from Gabaon unto Gazera.

text.NRSV: The fame of David went out into all lands, and the Lord brought the fear of him on all nations.
text.Rotherham: And the name of David went forth, throughout all the lands,—and, Yahweh, put the dread of him upon all the nations.
text.KJV: 14:17 And the fame of Dauid went out into all lands, and the Lord brought the feare of him vpon all nations.
text.DRV: 14:17 And the name of David was bruited in all countries, and our Lord gave the dread of him over all nations.

text.NRSV: David built houses for himself in the city of David, and he prepared a place for the ark of God and pitched a tent for it.
text.Rotherham: And he made him houses, in the city of David,—and prepared a place for the ark of God, and pitched for it a tent.
text.KJV: 15:1 And Dauid made him houses in the citie of Dauid, and prepared a place for the Arke of God, and pitched for it a tent.
text.DRV: 15:1 He made also houses for himself in the city of David: and built a place for the Ark of God, and pitcht a tabernacle for it.

text.NRSV: Then David said, “No one but the Levites may carry the ark of God, for the Lord chose them to carry the ark of the Lord and to minister to him forever.”
text.Rotherham: Then, said David, None must carry the ark of God, save the Levites,—for, of them, did Yahweh make choice, to bear the ark of Yahweh, and to attend it, unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: 15:2 Then Dauid sayd, None ought to carie the Arke of God, but the Leuites: for them hath the Lord chosen to cary the Arke of God, and to minister vnto him for euer.
text.DRV: 15:2 Then said David: It is unlawful that the Ark of God be carried of any man, but of the Levites: whom our Lord chose to carry it, and to minister unto himself for ever.

text.NRSV: David assembled all Israel in Jerusalem to bring up the ark of the Lord to its place, which he had prepared for it.
text.Rotherham: So David convoked all Israel, unto Jerusalem,—to bring up the ark of Yahweh, unto the place thereof, which he had prepared for it.
text.KJV: 15:3 And Dauid gathered all Israel together to Ierusalem, to bring vp the Arke of the Lord vnto his place, which hee had prepared for it.
text.DRV: 15:3 And he gathered together all Israel into Jerusalem, that the Ark of God might be brought into his place, which he had prepared for it.

text.NRSV: Then David gathered together the descendants of Aaron and the Levites:
text.Rotherham: And David gathered together the sons of Aaron, and the Levites:
text.KJV: 15:4 And Dauid assembled the children of Aaron, and the Leuites.
text.DRV: 15:4 Moreover also the sons of Aaron, and the Levites.

text.NRSV: of the sons of Kohath, Uriel the chief, with one hundred twenty of his kindred;
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Kohath, Uriel the chief, and his brethren, one hundred and twenty;
text.KJV: 15:5 Of the sonnes of Kohath: Uriel the chiefe, and his brethren an hundred and twentie.
text.DRV: 15:5 Of the children of Caath, Uriel was the Prince, and his brethren an hundred twenty.

text.NRSV: of the sons of Merari, Asaiah the chief, with two hundred twenty of his kindred;
text.Rotherham: of the sons of Merari, Asaiah the chief, and his brethren, two hundred and twenty;
text.KJV: 15:6 Of the sonnes of Merari: Asaiah the chiefe, and his brethren two hundred and twentie.
text.DRV: 15:6 Of the sons of Merari, Asaia the Prince: and his brethren two hundred twenty.

text.NRSV: of the sons of Gershom, Joel the chief, with one hundred thirty of his kindred;
text.Rotherham: of the sons of Gershom, Joel the chief, and his brethren, one hundred and thirty;
text.KJV: 15:7 Of the sonnes of Gershom: Ioel the chiefe, and his brethren an hundred and thirtie.
text.DRV: 15:7 Of the sons of Gersom, Joel the Prince: and his brethren an hundred thirty.

text.NRSV: of the sons of Elizaphan, Shemaiah the chief, with two hundred of his kindred;
text.Rotherham: of the sons of Elizaphan, Shemaiah the chief, and his brethren, two hundred;
text.KJV: 15:8 Of the sonnes of Elizaphan: Shemaiah the chiefe, and his brethren two hundred.
text.DRV: 15:8 Of the sons of Elisaphan, Semeias the Prince: and his brethren two hundred.

text.NRSV: of the sons of Hebron, Eliel the chief, with eighty of his kindred;
text.Rotherham: of the sons of Hebron, Eliel the chief, and his brethren, eighty;
text.KJV: 15:9 Of the sonnes of Hebron: Eliel the chiefe, and his brethren fourescore.
text.DRV: 15:9 Of the sons of Hebron, Eliel the Prince: and his brethren eighty.

text.NRSV: of the sons of Uzziel, Amminadab the chief, with one hundred twelve of his kindred.
text.Rotherham: of the son of Uzziel, Amminadab the chief, and his brethren, a hundred and twelve.
text.KJV: 15:10 Of the sonnes of Uzziel: Amminadab the chiefe, and his brethren an hundred and twelue.
text.DRV: 15:10 Of the sons of Oziel, Aminadab the Prince: and his brethren an hundred twelve.

text.NRSV: David summoned the priests Zadok and Abiathar, and the Levites Uriel, Asaiah, Joel, Shemaiah, Eliel, and Amminadab.
text.Rotherham: Then called David, for Zadok and for Abiathar, the priests,—and for the Levites, for Uriel, Asaiah and Joel, Shemaiah and Eliel, and Amminadab;
text.KJV: 15:11 And Dauid called for Zadok and Abiathar the Priests, and for the Leuites, for Uriel, Asaiah and Ioel, Shemaiah, and Eliel, and Amminadab,
text.DRV: 15:11 And David called Sadoc and Abiathar the Priests, and the Levites, Uriel, Asaia, Joel, Semeia, Eliel, and Aminadab:

text.NRSV: He said to them, “You are the heads of the ancestral houses of the Levites; sanctify yourselves, you and your kindred, so that you may bring up the ark of the Lord, the God of Israel, to the place that I have prepared for it.
text.Rotherham: and said unto them, Ye, are the chiefs of the fathers of the Levites,—Hallow yourselves, ye and your brethren, so shall ye bring up the ark of Yahweh—God of Israel, into [the place] I have prepared for it.
text.KJV: 15:12 And said vnto them, Yee are the chiefe of the fathers of the Leuites: sanctifie your selues both yee and your brethren, that you may bring vp the Arke of the Lord God of Israel, vnto the place that I haue prepared for it.
text.DRV: 15:12 and he said to them: You that are the Princes of the Levitical families, be sanctified with your brethren, and fetch the Ark of our Lord the God of Israel to the place, which is prepared for it:

text.NRSV: Because you did not carry it the first time, the Lord our God burst out against us, because we did not give it proper care.”
text.Rotherham: Because, at the first, ye, did not [bring it], Yahweh our God brake forth against us, because we sought him not in the appointed way.
text.KJV: 15:13 For because ye did it not at the first, the Lord our God made a breach vpon vs, for that we sought him not after the due order.
text.DRV: 15:13 lest as from the beginning, because you were not present, our Lord struck us: so now also it come to pass, we doing some unlawful thing.

text.NRSV: So the priests and the Levites sanctified themselves to bring up the ark of the Lord, the God of Israel.
text.Rotherham: So the priests and the Levites hallowed themselves,—to bring up the ark of Yahweh, God of Israel.
text.KJV: 15:14 So the Priestes and the Leuites sanctified themselues to bring vp the Arke of the Lord God of Israel.
text.DRV: 15:14 The Priests therefore, and the Levites were sanctified, to carry the Ark of our Lord the God of Israel.

text.NRSV: And the Levites carried the ark of God on their shoulders with the poles, as Moses had commanded according to the word of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of the Levites bare the ark of God, just as Moses commanded, according to the word of Yahweh,—on their shoulder, with the staves upon them.
text.KJV: 15:15 And the children of the Leuites bare the Arke of God vpon their shoulders, with the staues thereon, as Moses commanded, according to the word of the Lord.
text.DRV: 15:15 And the sons of Levi took the Ark of God, as Moyses had commanded, according to the word of our Lord, upon their shoulders, on bars.

text.NRSV: David also commanded the chiefs of the Levites to appoint their kindred as the singers to play on musical instruments, on harps and lyres and cymbals, to raise loud sounds of joy.
text.Rotherham: And David spake to the chiefs of the Levites, to station their brethren the singers, with instruments of song, harps and lyres, and cymbals,—sounding aloud at the lifting up of the voice, for joy.
text.KJV: 15:16 And Dauid spake to the chiefe of the Leuites, to appoint their brethren to be the singers with instruments of musicke, Psalteries, and Harpes, and Cymbales, sounding, by lifting vp the voice with ioy.
text.DRV: 15:16 And David said to the Princes of the Levites, that they should appoint of their brethren singing men on musical instruments, to wit, on nables, and harps, and cymbals, that the sound of joy might resound on high.

text.NRSV: So the Levites appointed Heman son of Joel; and of his kindred Asaph son of Berechiah; and of the sons of Merari, their kindred, Ethan son of Kushaiah;
text.Rotherham: So the Levites caused to stand, Heman son of Joel, and, of his brethren, Asaph, son of Berechiah,—and, of the sons of Merari their brethren, Ethan, son of Kushaiah;
text.KJV: 15:17 So the Leuites appointed Heman the sonne of Ioel: and of his brethren. Asaph the sonne of Berechiah: and of the sonnes of Merari their brethren, Ethan the sonne of Kushaiah.
text.DRV: 15:17 And they appointed Levites: Heman the son of Joel, and of his brethren Asaph the son of Barachias: and of the children of Merari, their brethren: Ethan the son of Casaia.

text.NRSV: and with them their kindred of the second order, Zechariah, Jaaziel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Unni, Eliab, Benaiah, Maaseiah, Mattithiah, Eliphelehu, and Mikneiah, and the gatekeepers Obed-edom and Jeiel.
text.Rotherham: and, with them, their brethren of the second degree,—Zechariah, Ben, and Jaaziel, and Shemiramoth, and Jehiel and Unni, Eliab, and Benaiah, and Maaseiah, and Mattithiah, and Eliphelehu, and Mickneiah, and Obed-edom and Jeiel, the door-keepers.
text.KJV: 15:18 And with them their brethren of the second degree, Zachariah, Ben, and Iaziel, & Shemiramoth, and Iehiel, and Unni, Eliab, and Benaiah, and Maasiah, and Mattithiah, and Eliphaleh, and Mikniah, and Obed Edom, and Iehiel the Porters.
text.DRV: 15:18 And with them their brethren: in the second order, Zacharias, & Ben, & Jaziel, and Semiramoth, and Jahiel, and Ani, Eliab, and Banaias, and Maasias, and Mathathias, and Eliphalu, and Macenias, and Obededom, and Jehiel, porters.

text.NRSV: The singers Heman, Asaph, and Ethan were to sound bronze cymbals;
text.Rotherham: And, the singers, Heman, Asaph and Ethan, with cymbals of bronze, were to sound aloud;
text.KJV: 15:19 So the Singers, Heman, Asaph, and Ethan, were appointed to sound with cymbales of brasse.
text.DRV: 15:19 Moreover them that sang, Heman, Asaph and Ethan: sounding on brazen cymbals.

text.NRSV: Zechariah, Aziel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Unni, Eliab, Maaseiah, and Benaiah were to play harps according to Alamoth;
text.Rotherham: and Zechariah, and Aziel, and Shemiramoth, and Jehiel, and Unni, and Eliab, and Maaseiah and Benaiah,—with harps, over the trebles;
text.KJV: 15:20 And Zachariah, and Aziel, and Shemiramoth, and Iehiel, and Unni, and Eliab, and Maasiah, and Benaiah, with Psalteries on Alamoth.
text.DRV: 15:20 And Zacharias, and Oziel, and Semiramoth, and Jahiel, and Ani, and Eliab, and Maasias, and Banaias upon nables sang mysteries.

text.NRSV: but Mattithiah, Eliphelehu, Mikneiah, Obed-edom, Jeiel, and Azaziah were to lead with lyres according to the Sheminith.
text.Rotherham: and Mattithiah, Eliphelehu, and Mikneiah, and Obed-edom, and Jeiel and Azaziah,—with lyres over the bass, to take the lead.
text.KJV: 15:21 And Mattithiah, and Eliphaleh, and Mikniah, and Obed Edom, and Ieiel, and Azzaziah, with harpes on the Sheminith to excell.
text.DRV: 15:21 Moreover Mathathias, and Eliphalu, and Macenias, and Obededom, and Jehiel, and Ozaziu, upon harps for the octave sang a triumphant song.

text.NRSV: Chenaniah, leader of the Levites in music, was to direct the music, for he understood it.
text.Rotherham: And, Chenaniah the leader of the Levites, in the service, used to give instructions in the service, because skilful was he.
text.KJV: 15:22 And Chenaniah chiefe of the Leuites was for song: he instructed about the song, because he was skilfull.
text.DRV: 15:22 And Chonenias the Prince of the Levites, was chief over Prophecy, to begin the melody: for he was very cunning.

text.NRSV: Berechiah and Elkanah were to be gatekeepers for the ark.
text.Rotherham: And, Berechiah and Elkanah, were door-keepers to the ark.
text.KJV: 15:23 And Berechiah, and Elkanah were doore keepers for the Arke.
text.DRV: 15:23 And Barachias, and Elcana: door keepers of the Ark.

text.NRSV: Shebaniah, Joshaphat, Nethanel, Amasai, Zechariah, Benaiah, and Eliezer, the priests, were to blow the trumpets before the ark of God. Obed-edom and Jehiah also were to be gatekeepers for the ark.
text.Rotherham: And, Shebaniah, and Joshaphat, and Nethanel, and Amasai, and Zechariah, and Benaiah, and Eliezer, the priests, were blowing with the trumpets, before the ark of God,—and, Obed-edom and Jehiah, were door-keepers, to the ark.
text.KJV: 15:24 And Shebaniah, and Iehoshaphat, and Nathaneel, and Amasai, and Zachariah, and Benaiah, and Eliezer the priests, did blow with the trumpets before the Arke of God: and Obed Edom, and Iehiah were doore keepers for the Arke.
text.DRV: 15:24 Moreover Sebenias, and Josaphat, and Nathanael, and Amasai, and Zacharias, and Banaias, and Eliezer Priests, sounded with trumpets, before the Ark of God: and Obededom, and Jehias kept the door of the Ark.

text.NRSV: So David and the elders of Israel and the commanders of thousands went to bring up the ark of the covenant of the Lord from the house of Obed-edom with rejoicing.
text.Rotherham: Thus, it was, David and the elders of Israel, and the rulers of thousands, who were going, to bring up the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, out of the house of Obed-edom, with joy.
text.KJV: 15:25 ¶ So Dauid and the Elders of Israel, and the captaines ouer thousands, went to bring vp the Arke of the couenant of the Lord, out of the house of Obed Edom with ioy.
text.DRV: 15:25 Therefore David and all the ancients of Israel, and the tribunes, went to fetch the Ark of the covenant of our Lord, out of the house of Obededom with joy.

text.NRSV: And because God helped the Levites who were carrying the ark of the covenant of the Lord, they sacrificed seven bulls and seven rams.
text.Rotherham: And so it was that, when God helped the Levites, who were bearing the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, they sacrificed seven bullocks and seven rams.
text.KJV: 15:26 And it came to passe when God helped the Leuites that bare the Arke of the couenant of the Lord, that they offered seuen bullocks, and seuen rammes.
text.DRV: 15:26 And when God had holpen the Levites, which carried the Ark of the covenant of our Lord, there were immolated seven oxen, and seven rams.

text.NRSV: David was clothed with a robe of fine linen, as also were all the Levites who were carrying the ark, and the singers, and Chenaniah the leader of the music of the singers; and David wore a linen ephod.
text.Rotherham: Now, David, was wrapped about with a robe of byssus, with all the Levites who were bearing the ark, and the singers, and Chenaniah the leader of the service rendered by the singers,—but, upon David, was an ephod of white linen.
text.KJV: 15:27 And Dauid was clothed with a robe of fine linnen, and all the Leuites that bare the Arke, and the singers, and Chenaniah the master of the song, with the singers. Dauid also had vpon him, an Ephod of linnen.
text.DRV: 15:27 Moreover David was clothed with a robe of fine linen, & all the Levites that carried the Ark, and the singing men, and Chonenias the Prince of prophecy among the singers: and David also was clothed with an Ephod of linen.

text.NRSV: So all Israel brought up the ark of the covenant of the Lord with shouting, to the sound of the horn, trumpets, and cymbals, playing loudly on harps and lyres.
text.Rotherham: So, all Israel, were bringing up the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, with shouting, and with sound of the horn, and with trumpets, and with cymbals,—sounding aloud, with harps, and lyres.
text.KJV: 15:28 Thus all Israel brought vp the Arke of the Couenant of the Lord with shouting, and with sound of the cornet, and with trumpets, and with cymbals, making a noise with psalteries and harpes.
text.DRV: 15:28 And all Israel brought the Ark of the covenant of our Lord in jubilee, and sounding with the sound of shalm, and with trumpets, and cymbals, and nables, and harps.

text.NRSV: As the ark of the covenant of the Lord came to the city of David, Michal daughter of Saul looked out of the window, and saw King David leaping and dancing; and she despised him in her heart.
text.Rotherham: And so it was that, when the ark of the covenant of Yahweh came in as far as the city of David,—and Michal daughter of Saul looked forth through the window, and saw King David, dancing and playing, that she despised him in her heart.
text.KJV: 15:29 ¶ And it came to passe as the Arke of the couenant of the Lord came to the citie of Dauid, that Michal the daughter of Saul looking out at a window, saw King Dauid dauncing and playing: and shee despised him in her heart.
text.DRV: 15:29 And when the Ark of the covenant of our Lord was come unto the city of David, Michol the daughter of Saul looking forth through a window, saw David the king dancing and playing, and she despised him in her heart.

text.NRSV: They brought the ark of God, and set it inside the tent that David had pitched for it; and they offered burnt offerings and offerings of well-being before God.
text.Rotherham: So they brought in the ark of God, and placed it in the midst of the tent, which David had pitched for it,—and they brought near ascending-sacrifices and peace-offerings, before God.
text.KJV: 16:1 So they brought the Arke of God, and set it in the midst of the tent that Dauid had pitched for it: and they offered burnt sacrifices, and peace offerings before God.
text.DRV: 16:1 They therefore brought the Ark of God, and set it in the midst of the tabernacle, which David had pitched for it: and they offered holocausts, and pacifics before God.

text.NRSV: When David had finished offering the burnt offerings and the offerings of well-being, he blessed the people in the name of the Lord;
text.Rotherham: And, when David had made an end of offering the ascending-sacrifices, and the peace-offerings, he blessed the people, in the name of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 16:2 And when Dauid had made an end of offering the burnt offerings, and the peace offrings, he blessed the people in the name of the Lord.
text.DRV: 16:2 And when David had finished offering holocausts, and pacifics, he blessed the people in the name of our Lord.

text.NRSV: and he distributed to every person in Israel—man and woman alike—to each a loaf of bread, a portion of meat, and a cake of raisins.
text.Rotherham: And he apportioned, to every one of Israel, both man and woman,—to every one, a loaf of bread, and a sweet drink, and a raisin cake.
text.KJV: 16:3 And hee dealt to euery one of Israel, both man and woman, to euery one a loafe of bread, and a good piece of flesh, and a flagon of wine.
text.DRV: 16:3 And he divided to all throughout every one, from man unto woman, a loaf of bread, and a piece of roasted beef, and flour fried with oil.

text.NRSV: He appointed certain of the Levites as ministers before the ark of the Lord, to invoke, to thank, and to praise the Lord, the God of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And he placed before the ark of Yahweh certain of the Levites, as attendants,—and to celebrate, and to give thanks and render praise, unto Yahweh, God of Israel;
text.KJV: 16:4 ¶ And he appointed certaine of the Leuites to minister before the Arke of the Lord, and to record, and to thanke and praise the Lord God of Israel,
text.DRV: 16:4 And he appointed before the Ark of our Lord of the Levites, that should minister, and should remember his works, and glorify, and praise our Lord the God of Israel:

text.NRSV: Asaph was the chief, and second to him Zechariah, Jeiel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Mattithiah, Eliab, Benaiah, Obed-edom, and Jeiel, with harps and lyres; Asaph was to sound the cymbals,
text.Rotherham: Asaph the chief, and, second to him, Zechariah,—Jeiel, and Shemiramoth, and Jehiel, and Mattithiah, and Eliab, and Benaiah, and Obed-edom, and Jeiel, with instruments of harps, and with lyres, and Asaph with cymbals, sounding aloud:
text.KJV: 16:5 Asaph the chiefe, and next to him Zachariah, Ieiel, and Shemiramoth, and Iehiel, and Mattithiah, and Eliab, and Benaiah, and Obed Edom: and Ieiel with Psalteries and with harpes: but Asaph made a sound with cymbals.
text.DRV: 16:5 Asaph the Prince, and the second after him Zacharias: moreover Jahiel, and Semiramoth, and Jehiel, and Mathathias, and Eliab, and Banaias, and Obededom: and Jehiel over the instruments of psaltery, and the harps: and Asaph to sound upon the cymbals:

text.NRSV: and the priests Benaiah and Jahaziel were to blow trumpets regularly before the ark of the covenant of God.
text.Rotherham: and Benaiah and Jahaziel, the priests,—with trumpets continually, before the ark of the covenant of God.
text.KJV: 16:6 Benaiah also and Iahaziel the Priestes, with trumpets continually before the Arke of the Couenant of God.
text.DRV: 16:6 but Banaias, and Jaziel Priests, to sound the trumpet continually before the Ark of the covenant of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Then on that day David first appointed the singing of praises to the Lord by Asaph and his kindred.
text.Rotherham: On that day, then, David gave [these songs] in chief, to give thanks unto Yahweh,—through Asaph, and his brethren:—
text.KJV: 16:7 ¶ Then on that day, Dauid deliuered first this Psalme to thanke the Lord, into the hand of Asaph and his brethren:
text.DRV: 16:7 In that day David made Asaph, prince to confess to our Lord and his brethren.

text.NRSV: O give thanks to the Lord, call on his name, make known his deeds among the peoples.
text.Rotherham: Give ye thanks to Yahweh, Call upon his name, Make known among the peoples, his doings;
text.KJV: 16:8 Giue thankes vnto the Lord, call vpon his name, make knowen his deeds among the people.
text.DRV: 16:8 Confess ye to our Lord, and invocate his name: make his inventions known among the peoples.

text.NRSV: Sing to him, sing praises to him, tell of all his wonderful works.
text.Rotherham: Sing ye to him, Make music unto him, Speak ye of all his wonders.
text.KJV: 16:9 Sing vnto him, sing psalmes vnto him, talke you of all his wonderous workes.
text.DRV: 16:9 Chant to him, and sing to him: & tell ye all his marvelous things.

text.NRSV: Glory in his holy name; let the hearts of those who seek the Lord rejoice.
text.Rotherham: Boast yourselves in his holy name, Joyful be the heart of them who are seeking Yahweh.
text.KJV: 16:10 Glory yee in his holy Name, let the heart of them reioyce that seeke the Lord.
text.DRV: 16:10 Praise ye his holy name: let the heart of them rejoice, that seek our Lord.

text.NRSV: Seek the Lord and his strength, seek his presence continually.
text.Rotherham: Search out Yahweh, and his power, Seek diligently his face, at all times.
text.KJV: 16:11 Seeke the Lord, and his strength, seeke his face continually.
text.DRV: 16:11 Seek ye our Lord, and his power: seek ye his face always.

text.NRSV: Remember the wonderful works he has done, his miracles, and the judgments he uttered,
text.Rotherham: Remember his wonders which he hath done, His splendid deeds, and the just decisions of his mouth.
text.KJV: 16:12 Remember his marueilous works that he hath done, his wonders, and the iudgements of his mouth,
text.DRV: 16:12 Remember his marvelous things which he hath done: his signs, and the judgements of his mouth.

text.NRSV: O offspring of his servant Israel, children of Jacob, his chosen ones.
text.Rotherham: O ye seed of Israel his servant, sons of Jacob his chosen ones:
text.KJV: 16:13 O ye seed of Israel his seruant, ye children of Iacob his chosen ones.
text.DRV: 16:13 The seed of Israel his servant: the children of Jacob his elect.

text.NRSV: He is the Lord our God; his judgments are in all the earth.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh himself, is our God, through all the land, are his just decisions.
text.KJV: 16:14 He is the Lord our God, his iudgements are in all the earth.
text.DRV: 16:14 He is the Lord our God: in all the earth are his judgements.

text.NRSV: Remember his covenant forever, the word that he commanded, for a thousand generations,
text.Rotherham: Remember yea to times age-abiding, his covenant, the word he hath commanded, to a thousand generations;
text.KJV: 16:15 Be ye mindfull alwayes of his Couenant: the worde which hee commanded to a thousand generations:
text.DRV: 16:15 Remember for ever his covenant: the word, which he commanded unto a thousand generations.

text.NRSV: the covenant that he made with Abraham, his sworn promise to Isaac,
text.Rotherham: Which he solemnised with Abraham, and his oath, to Isaac;
text.KJV: 16:16 Euen of the Couenant which hee made with Abraham, and of his othe vnto Isaac:
text.DRV: 16:16 Which he covenanted with Abraham: and his oath with Isaac.

text.NRSV: which he confirmed to Jacob as a statute, to Israel as an everlasting covenant,
text.Rotherham: And confirmed it, unto Jacob, for a statute, unto Israel, as a covenant age-abiding:
text.KJV: 16:17 And hath confirmed the same to Iacob for a lawe, and to Israel for an euerlasting Couenant,
text.DRV: 16:17 And he appointed it to Jacob for a precept: and to Israel for an everlasting covenant:

text.NRSV: saying, “To you I will give the land of Canaan as your portion for an inheritance.”
text.Rotherham: Saying, To thee, will I give the land of Canaan, as your inherited portion:
text.KJV: 16:18 Saying, vnto thee will I giue the land of Canaan, the lot of your inheritance.
text.DRV: 16:18 Saying: To thee will I give the Land of Chanaan: the cord of your inheritance.

text.NRSV: When they were few in number, of little account, and strangers in the land,
text.Rotherham: While, as yet, ye were men who could be counted, a very few, and sojourners therein:
text.KJV: 16:19 When ye were but few, euen a few, and strangers in it:
text.DRV: 16:19 When they were few in number: small and sojourners thereof.

text.NRSV: wandering from nation to nation, from one kingdom to another people,
text.Rotherham: And they wandered from nation to nation, and from one kingdom, to another people:
text.KJV: 16:20 And when they went from nation to nation, and from one kingdome to another people:
text.DRV: 16:20 And they passed from nation into nation: and from Kingdom to another people.

text.NRSV: he allowed no one to oppress them; he rebuked kings on their account,
text.Rotherham: He suffered no man to oppress them, And reproved—for their sakes—kings!
text.KJV: 16:21 Hee suffered no man to doe them wrong: yea, hee reprooued kings for their sakes,
text.DRV: 16:21 He suffered not any man to calumniate them: but rebuked kings for their sake.

text.NRSV: saying, “Do not touch my anointed ones; do my prophets no harm.”
text.Rotherham: Ye may not touch mine Anointed ones, And, to my Prophets, may ye do no wrong.
text.KJV: 16:22 Saying, Touch not mine anointed, and doe my Prophets no harme.
text.DRV: 16:22 Touch not my anointed: and unto my Prophets be not malicious.

text.NRSV: Sing to the Lord, all the earth. Tell of his salvation from day to day.
text.Rotherham: Sing to Yahweh, all the earth, Tell the tidings, from day to day, of his salvation:
text.KJV: 16:23 Sing vnto the Lord all the earth: shew foorth from day to day his saluation.
text.DRV: 16:23 Sing ye to our Lord all the earth: shew forth from day to day his salvation.

text.NRSV: Declare his glory among the nations, his marvelous works among all the peoples.
text.Rotherham: Recount, among the nations, his glory, among all the peoples, his wonders.
text.KJV: 16:24 Declare his glory among the heathen: his marueilous workes among all nations.
text.DRV: 16:24 Tell his glory among the gentiles: among all peoples his marvelous works.

text.NRSV: For great is the Lord, and greatly to be praised; he is to be revered above all gods.
text.Rotherham: For great is Yahweh, and worthy to be mightily praised, and to be revered is he above all gods;
text.KJV: 16:25 For great is the Lord, and greatly to be praised: he also is to be feared aboue all gods.
text.DRV: 16:25 Because our Lord is great, and laudable exceedingly: and terrible over all gods.

text.NRSV: For all the gods of the peoples are idols, but the Lord made the heavens.
text.Rotherham: For, all the gods of the peoples, are things of nought, but, Yahweh, made, the heavens.
text.KJV: 16:26 For all the gods of the people are idoles: but the Lord made the heauens.
text.DRV: 16:26 For all the gods of the peoples, be idols: but our Lord made the heavens.

text.NRSV: Honor and majesty are before him; strength and joy are in his place.
text.Rotherham: Praise and majesty, are before him, Strength and joy, are in his dwelling-place.
text.KJV: 16:27 Glory and honour are in his presence: strength and gladnesse are in his place.
text.DRV: 16:27 Confession and magnificence before him: strength and joy in his place.

text.NRSV: Ascribe to the Lord, O families of the peoples, ascribe to the Lord glory and strength.
text.Rotherham: Give to Yahweh, ye families of the peoples, Give to Yahweh, glory and strength:
text.KJV: 16:28 Giue vnto the Lord, yee kinreds of the people: giue vnto the Lord glory and strength.
text.DRV: 16:28 Bring to our Lord ye families of peoples: bring to our Lord glory and empire.

text.NRSV: Ascribe to the Lord the glory due his name; bring an offering, and come before him. Worship the Lord in holy splendor;
text.Rotherham: Give to Yahweh, the glory of his name,—Bring ye a present, and enter before him, Bow down to Yahweh, in the adornment of holiness:
text.KJV: 16:29 Giue vnto the Lord the glory due vnto his Name: bring an offering, and come before him, worship the Lord in the beautie of holinesse.
text.DRV: 16:29 Give our Lord glory, to his name, elevate sacrifice, and come ye in his sight: and adore our Lord in holy honour.

text.NRSV: tremble before him, all the earth. The world is firmly established; it shall never be moved.
text.Rotherham: Tremble at his presence, all the earth, But the world, shall be established, it shall not be shaken.
text.KJV: 16:30 Feare before him all the earth: the world also shall be stable, that it be not mooued.
text.DRV: 16:30 Let all the earth be moved before his face: for he founded the world unmovable.

text.NRSV: Let the heavens be glad, and let the earth rejoice, and let them say among the nations, “The Lord is king!”
text.Rotherham: Let the heavens rejoice, and the earth exult, Let them say among the nations, Yahweh, hath become king!
text.KJV: 16:31 Let the heauens be glad, and let the earth reioyce: and let men say among the nations, The Lord reigneth.
text.DRV: 16:31 Let the heavens be glad, and the earth rejoice: and let them say among nations, Our Lord hath reigned.

text.NRSV: Let the sea roar, and all that fills it; let the field exult, and everything in it.
text.Rotherham: Let the sea roar, and the fulness thereof, Let the field leap for joy, and all that is therein:
text.KJV: 16:32 Let the sea roare, and the fulnesse thereof: let the fieldes reioyce, and all that is therein.
text.DRV: 16:32 Let the sea thunder, and the fullness thereof: let the fields rejoice, and all things that are in them.

text.NRSV: Then shall the trees of the forest sing for joy before the Lord, for he comes to judge the earth.
text.Rotherham: Then, shall shout in triumph, the trees of the forest, before Yahweh, for he is coming, to judge the earth.
text.KJV: 16:33 Then shall the trees of the wood sing out at the presence of the Lord, because hee commeth to iudge the earth.
text.DRV: 16:33 Then shall the trees of the forest praise before our Lord: because he is come to judge the earth.

text.NRSV: O give thanks to the Lord, for he is good; for his steadfast love endures forever.
text.Rotherham: Give ye thanks unto Yahweh, For he is good, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
text.KJV: 16:34 O giue thanks vnto the Lord, for hee is good: for his mercy endureth for euer.
text.DRV: 16:34 Confess ye to our Lord, because he is good: because his mercy is for ever.

text.NRSV: Say also: “Save us, O God of our salvation, and gather and rescue us from among the nations, that we may give thanks to your holy name, and glory in your praise.
text.Rotherham: And say ye, Save us, O God of our salvation, and gather us and deliver us, from among the nations,—that we may give thanks unto thy holy name, that we may triumph aloud in thy praise.
text.KJV: 16:35 And say yee, Saue vs, O God of our saluation, and gather vs together, and deliuer vs from the heathen, that we may giue thanks to thy holy Name, and glory in thy praise.
text.DRV: 16:35 And say ye: Save us o God our Saviour: and gather us together, and deliver us out of the nations, that we may confess to thy holy name, and may rejoice in thy songs.

text.NRSV: Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting.” Then all the people said “Amen!” and praised the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Blessed be Yahweh, God of Israel, from one age even to another age. Then said all the people, Amen, And gave praise to Yahweh.
text.KJV: 16:36 Blessed be the Lord God of Israel for euer and euer: and all the people saide, Amen, and praised the Lord.
text.DRV: 16:36 Blessed be our Lord the God of Israel from eternity unto eternity: and let all the people say: Amen, and hymn to God.

text.NRSV: David left Asaph and his kindred there before the ark of the covenant of the Lord to minister regularly before the ark as each day required,
text.Rotherham: So he left there, before the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, Asaph and his brethren,—to be in attendance before the ark continually, for the duty of a day on its day;
text.KJV: 16:37 ¶ So hee left there before the Arke of the couenant of the Lord, Asaph and his brethren, to minister before the Arke continually, as euery dayes worke required:
text.DRV: 16:37 He therefore left Asaph there before the Ark of the covenant of our Lord, and his brethren to minister in the presence of the Ark continually day by day, & in their courses.

text.NRSV: and also Obed-edom and his sixty-eight kindred; while Obed-edom son of Jeduthun and Hosah were to be gatekeepers.
text.Rotherham: and Obed-edom with his brethren, sixty-eight,—and Obed-edom son of Jedithun and Hosah to be doorkeepers;
text.KJV: 16:38 And Obed Edom with their brethren, threescore and eight: Obed Edom also the sonne of Ieduthun, and Hosah to be porters:
text.DRV: 16:38 Moreover Obededom, and his brethren sixty eight: and Obededom the son of Idithun, and Hosa he appointed for porters.

text.NRSV: And he left the priest Zadok and his kindred the priests before the tabernacle of the Lord in the high place that was at Gibeon,
text.Rotherham: and Zadok the priest, and his brethren the priests, before the habitation of Yahweh,—in the high place that was in Gibeon:
text.KJV: 16:39 And Zadok the Priest, and his brethren the Priests, before the Tabernacle of the Lord, in the high place that was at Gibeon,
text.DRV: 16:39 And Sadoc the Priest, and his brethren Priests, before the tabernacle of our Lord in the excelse, which was in Gabaon,

text.NRSV: to offer burnt offerings to the Lord on the altar of burnt offering regularly, morning and evening, according to all that is written in the law of the Lord that he commanded Israel.
text.Rotherham: to offer ascending-sacrifices unto Yahweh, upon the altar of ascending-sacrifice,—continually, morning and evening,—even according to all that is written in the law of Yahweh, which he commanded concerning Israel;
text.KJV: 16:40 To offer burnt offerings vnto the Lord, vpon the Altar of the burnt offering continually morning and euening, and to doe according to all that is written in the Lawe of the Lord, which hee commanded Israel:
text.DRV: 16:40 that they should offer holocausts to our Lord upon the altar of holocaust continually, morning and evening, according to all things that are written in the law of our Lord, which he commanded Israel.

text.NRSV: With them were Heman and Jeduthun, and the rest of those chosen and expressly named to render thanks to the Lord, for his steadfast love endures forever.
text.Rotherham: and, with them, Heman and Jeduthun, and the rest of the chosen ones, who were distinguished by name,—to give thanks unto Yahweh, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness!
text.KJV: 16:41 And with them Heman and Ieduthun, and the rest that were chosen, who were expressed by name, to giue thankes to the Lord, because his mercy endureth for euer.
text.DRV: 16:41 And after him Heman, and Idithun, and the rest of the chosen men, every one by his name to confess unto our Lord: because his mercy is for ever.

text.NRSV: Heman and Jeduthun had with them trumpets and cymbals for the music, and instruments for sacred song. The sons of Jeduthun were appointed to the gate.
text.Rotherham: And, with them, Heman and Jeduthun, [with] the trumpets, and the cymbals, for sounding aloud, and the instruments for the songs of God,—and the sons of Jeduthun, for the gate.
text.KJV: 16:42 And with them Heman and Ieduthun with trumpets and cymbales, for those that should make a sound, and with musicall instruments of God: and the sonnes of Ieduthun were Porters.
text.DRV: 16:42 Heman also and Idithun sounding the trumpet, and quavering on the cymbals, and all musical instruments to sing unto God: and the sons of Idithun he made porters.

text.NRSV: Then all the people departed to their homes, and David went home to bless his household.
text.Rotherham: And all the people departed, every man to his house,—and David went round, to bless his own household.
text.KJV: 16:43 And all the people departed euery man to his house, and Dauid returned to blesse his house.
text.DRV: 16:43 And all the people returned into their house: and David, to bless also his house.

text.NRSV: Now when David settled in his house, David said to the prophet Nathan, “I am living in a house of cedar, but the ark of the covenant of the Lord is under a tent.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when David had taken up his abode in his house, that David said unto Nathan the prophet, Lo! I, am dwelling in a house of cedars, but, the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, is under curtains.
text.KJV: 17:1 Now it came to passe, as Dauid sate in his house, that Dauid sayde to Nathan the Prophet, Loe, I dwell in an house of Cedars, but the Arke of the Couenant of the Lord remaineth vnder curtaines.
text.DRV: 17:1 And when David dwelt in his house, he said to Nathan the Prophet: Behold I dwell in a house of cedar: and the Ark of the covenant of our Lord is under skins.

text.NRSV: Nathan said to David, “Do all that you have in mind, for God is with you.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Nathan unto David, All that is in thy heart, do,—for, God, is with thee.
text.KJV: 17:2 Then Nathan sayd vnto Dauid, Doe all that is in thine heart, for God is with thee.
text.DRV: 17:2 And Nathan said to David: All things, that are in thy heart do: for God is with thee.

text.NRSV: But that same night the word of the Lord came to Nathan, saying:
text.Rotherham: But so it was that, in that night, the word of God came unto Nathan, saying:
text.KJV: 17:3 ¶ And it came to passe the same night, that the word of God came to Nathan, saying,
text.DRV: 17:3 Therefore that night the word of God came to Nathan, saying:

text.NRSV: “Go and tell my servant David: Thus says the Lord: You shall not build me a house to live in.
text.Rotherham: Go and say unto David my servant, Thus, saith Yahweh,—Thou, shalt not build me a house, to dwell in;
text.KJV: 17:4 Goe and tell Dauid my seruant, Thus saith the Lord, Thou shalt not build me an house to dwell in.
text.DRV: 17:4 Go, and speak to David my servant: Thus saith our Lord: Thou shalt not build me a house to dwell in.

text.NRSV: For I have not lived in a house since the day I brought out Israel to this day, but I have gone from tent to tent and from dwelling to dwelling.
text.Rotherham: seeing that I have not dwelt in a house, since the day that I brought up Israel, until this day,—but have been from tent to tent, and without a habitation.
text.KJV: 17:5 For I haue not dwelt in a house since the day that I brought vp Israel, vnto this day, but haue gone from tent to tent, and from one Tabernacle to another.
text.DRV: 17:5 For neither have I remained in house from the time, that I brought out Israel, until this day: but I have been always changing places of tabernacle, & in tent,

text.NRSV: In all the places where I have moved about among all Israel, did I ever speak a word with any of the judges of Israel, whom I commanded to shepherd my people, saying, ‘Why have you not built me a house of cedar?’
text.Rotherham: Wheresoever I have wandered with all Israel, spake I ever, a word, with one of the judges of Israel, whom I charged to shepherd my people, saying,—Wherefore have ye not built me a house of cedars?
text.KJV: 17:6 Wheresoeuer I haue walked with all Israel, spake I a word to any of the Iudges of Israel (whom I commanded to feed my people) saying, Why haue ye not built me an house of Cedars?
text.DRV: 17:6 abiding with all Israel. Did I speak to one, at the least, of all the judges of Israel whom I commanded to feed my people, and did I say: Why have you not built me a house of cedar?

text.NRSV: Now therefore thus you shall say to my servant David: Thus says the Lord of hosts: I took you from the pasture, from following the sheep, to be ruler over my people Israel;
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, thus, shalt thou say unto my servant David, Thus, saith Yahweh of hosts, I myself, took thee from the pasture, from after the flock,—to become leader over my people Israel;
text.KJV: 17:7 Now therefore thus shalt thou say vnto my seruant Dauid, Thus saith the Lord of hosts, I tooke thee from the Sheep-coat, euen from folowing the sheep, that thou shouldest be ruler ouer my people Israel:
text.DRV: 17:7 Now therefore so shalt thou speak to my servant David: Thus saith the Lord of hosts: I took thee, when in the pastures thou didest follow the flock, that thou shouldest be Prince of my people Israel.

text.NRSV: and I have been with you wherever you went, and have cut off all your enemies from before you; and I will make for you a name, like the name of the great ones of the earth.
text.Rotherham: and was with thee whithersoever thou didst go, and have cut off all thine enemies, from before thee,—and will make thee a name, like the name of the great ones, who are in the earth;
text.KJV: 17:8 And I haue bene with thee whithersoeuer thou hast walked, and haue cut off all thine enemies from before thee, and haue made thee a name, like the name of the great men that are in the earth.
text.DRV: 17:8 And I have been with thee whither soever thou wentest: and I have slain all thine enemies before thee, and have made thee a name as of one of the great ones, that are renowned in the earth.

text.NRSV: I will appoint a place for my people Israel, and will plant them, so that they may live in their own place, and be disturbed no more; and evildoers shall wear them down no more, as they did formerly,
text.Rotherham: and will appoint a place for my people Israel, and will plant them, and they shall inhabit their place, and be unsettled no more,—neither shall the sons of perversity again wear them out, as at the first;
text.KJV: 17:9 Also I will ordeine a place for my people Israel, and will plant them, and they shall dwell in their place, and shall be moued no more: neither shal the children of wickednesse waste them any more (as at the beginning,
text.DRV: 17:9 And I have given a place to my people Israel: it shall be planted, and shall dwell therein, and shall be moved no more, neither shall the children of iniquity consume them, as from the beginning,

text.NRSV: from the time that I appointed judges over my people Israel; and I will subdue all your enemies. Moreover I declare to you that the Lord will build you a house.
text.Rotherham: even from the days when I put judges in charge over my people Israel, and have subdued all thine enemies,—that I might make thee great, yea, a house, will Yahweh build for thee.
text.KJV: 17:10 And since the time that I commanded Iudges to bee ouer my people Israel.) Moreouer, I will subdue all thine enemies. Furthermore I tel thee, that the Lord will build thee an house.
text.DRV: 17:10 since the days that I gave judges to my people Israel, & humbled all thine enemies. I therefore tell thee, that our Lord will build thee a house.

text.NRSV: When your days are fulfilled to go to be with your ancestors, I will raise up your offspring after you, one of your own sons, and I will establish his kingdom.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be that, when thy days are fulfilled, to sleep with thy fathers, then will I raise up thy seed after thee, who shall be of thy sons,—and I will establish his kingdom.
text.KJV: 17:11 ¶ And it shall come to passe, when thy dayes be expired, that thou must go to be with thy fathers, that I will raise vp thy seed after thee, which shall bee of thy sonnes, and I wil stablish his kingdome.
text.DRV: 17:11 And when thou shalt have accomplished thy days to go to thy fathers, I will raise up thy seed after thee, which shall be of thy children: and I will establish his Kingdom.

text.NRSV: He shall build a house for me, and I will establish his throne forever.
text.Rotherham: He, shall build me a house,—and I will establish his throne unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: 17:12 He shall build me an house, and I will stablish his throne for euer.
text.DRV: 17:12 He shall build me a house, and I will confirm his throne for ever.

text.NRSV: I will be a father to him, and he shall be a son to me. I will not take my steadfast love from him, as I took it from him who was before you,
text.Rotherham: I, will become his father, and, he, shall become my son,—and, my lovingkindness, will I not cause to depart from him,—as I caused it to depart from him who was before thee;
text.KJV: 17:13 I will be his father, and he shall be my sonne, and I will not take my mercie away from him, as I tooke it from him that was before thee.
text.DRV: 17:13 I will be to him for a father, and he shall be to me for a son: and my mercy I will not take from him, as I took from him, that was before thee.

text.NRSV: but I will confirm him in my house and in my kingdom forever, and his throne shall be established forever.”
text.Rotherham: but I will cause him to remain over my house and over my kingdom, unto times age-abiding,—and, his throne, shall be established, unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: 17:14 But I will settle him in mine house, and in my kingdom for euer, and his throne shall bee established for euermore.
text.DRV: 17:14 And I will establish him in my house, and in my Kingdom for ever: and his throne shall be most firm for ever.

text.NRSV: In accordance with all these words and all this vision, Nathan spoke to David.
text.Rotherham: According to all these words, and according to all this vision, so, spake Nathan unto David.
text.KJV: 17:15 According to all these words, and according to all this vision, so did Nathan speake vnto Dauid.
text.DRV: 17:15 According to all these words, and according to all this vision, so spake Nathan to David.

text.NRSV: Then King David went in and sat before the Lord, and said, “Who am I, O Lord God, and what is my house, that you have brought me thus far?
text.Rotherham: Then entered King David, and tarried before Yahweh,—and said, Who am, I, O Yahweh God, and what is my house, that thou hast brought me hitherto;
text.KJV: 17:16 ¶ And Dauid the king came, and sate before the Lord, and said, Who am I, O Lord God, and what is mine house, that thou hast brought mee hitherto?
text.DRV: 17:16 And when king David came, and sat before our Lord, he said: Who am I Lord God, and what is my house, that thou shouldest give me such things?

text.NRSV: And even this was a small thing in your sight, O God; you have also spoken of your servant’s house for a great while to come. You regard me as someone of high rank, O Lord God!
text.Rotherham: and hast made this seem little in thine eyes, O God, in that thou hast spoken concerning the house of thy servant, for a great while to come? Thus wilt thou provide for me, according to the rank of manhood, and hast exalted me, O Yahweh God!
text.KJV: 17:17 And yet this was a small thing in thine eyes, O God: for thou hast also spoken of thy seruants house, for a great while to come, and hast regarded mee according to the estate of a man of high degree, O Lord God.
text.DRV: 17:17 But this also hath seemed little in thy sight, and therefore thou hast spoken concerning the house of thy servant for time to come also: and hast made me renowned above all men Lord God.

text.NRSV: And what more can David say to you for honoring your servant? You know your servant.
text.Rotherham: What, yet further, can David say unto thee, [thus] to honour thy servant,—seeing that, thou thyself, knowest, thine own servant?
text.KJV: 17:18 What can Dauid speake more to thee for the honour of thy seruant? for thou knowest thy seruant.
text.DRV: 17:18 What can David add farther, whereas thou hast so glorified thy servant, and known him?

text.NRSV: For your servant’s sake, O Lord, and according to your own heart, you have done all these great deeds, making known all these great things.
text.Rotherham: O Yahweh, for the sake of thy servant, and according to thine own heart, hast thou done all this great thing,—making known all the great things.
text.KJV: 17:19 O Lord, for thy seruants sake, and according to thine owne heart, hast thou done all this greatnesse in making knowen all these great things.
text.DRV: 17:19 Lord for thy servant according to thy heart thou hast done all this magnificence, and would have all thy great wonders to be known.

text.NRSV: There is no one like you, O Lord, and there is no God besides you, according to all that we have heard with our ears.
text.Rotherham: O Yahweh, there is none like unto thee, yea there is no God besides thee, according to all that we have heard with our ears.
text.KJV: 17:20 O Lord, there is none like thee neither is there any God besides thee, according to all that we haue heard with our eares.
text.DRV: 17:20 Lord, there is not the like to thee: and there is none other beside thee, of all whom we have heard with our ears.

text.NRSV: Who is like your people Israel, one nation on the earth whom God went to redeem to be his people, making for yourself a name for great and terrible things, in driving out nations before your people whom you redeemed from Egypt?
text.Rotherham: Who then is like thy people Israel, a nation alone in the earth,—whom God went to redeem, to be his own people, to make for thyself a name for great and fearful things, to drive out, nations, from before thy people, whom thou didst redeem out of Egypt;
text.KJV: 17:21 And what one nation in the earth is like thy people Israel, whome God went to redeeme to be his owne people, to make thee a name of greatnesse and terriblenesse, by driuing out nations from before thy people whom thou hast redeemed out of Egypt?
text.DRV: 17:21 For what other is there, as thy people Israel, one nation in the earth, to the which God went, to deliver it and make it his people, and with his greatness & terrors cast out the nations before the face of it, which he delivered out of Ægypt?

text.NRSV: And you made your people Israel to be your people forever; and you, O Lord, became their God.
text.Rotherham: and didst appoint thy people Israel for thyself as a people, unto times age-abiding,—thou thyself, also, O Yahweh, becoming their God?
text.KJV: 17:22 For thy people Israel didst thou make thine owne people for euer, and thou, Lord, becamest their God.
text.DRV: 17:22 And thou hast made thy people Israel to be thy people for ever, and thou Lord art made the God thereof.

text.NRSV: “And now, O Lord, as for the word that you have spoken concerning your servant and concerning his house, let it be established forever, and do as you have promised.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, O Yahweh, the word which thou hast spoken concerning thy servant, and concerning his house, be it steadfast unto times age-abiding,—and do as thou hast spoken:
text.KJV: 17:23 Therefore now Lord, let the thing that thou hast spoken concerning thy seruant, and concerning his house, be established for euer, and doe as thou hast said.
text.DRV: 17:23 Now therefore Lord, the word, which thou hast spoken to thy servant, and concerning his house, be it confirmed for ever, and do as thou hast spoken.

text.NRSV: Thus your name will be established and magnified forever in the saying, ‘The Lord of hosts, the God of Israel, is Israel’s God’; and the house of your servant David will be established before you.
text.Rotherham: yea let it be steadfast, and so let thy Name be magnified unto times age-abiding, saying, Yahweh of hosts, the God of Israel, is a God to Israel! seeing that, the house of David thy servant, is established before thee.
text.KJV: 17:24 Let it euen bee established, that thy name may bee magnified for euer, saying, The Lord of hosts is the God of Israel, euen a God to Israel: and let the house of Dauid thy seruant be established before thee.
text.DRV: 17:24 And let thy name remain and be magnified for ever: and let it be said: The Lord of hosts is God of Israel, & the house of David his servant permanent before him.

text.NRSV: For you, my God, have revealed to your servant that you will build a house for him; therefore your servant has found it in his heart to pray before you.
text.Rotherham: For, thou, O my God, hast unveiled the ear of thy servant, that thou wilt build for him a house,—for this cause, hath thy servant found [it in his heart] to pray before thee.
text.KJV: 17:25 For thou, O my God, hast tolde thy seruant that thou wilt build him an house: therefore thy seruant hath found in his heart to pray before thee.
text.DRV: 17:25 For thou Lord my God hast revealed the ear of thy servant, to build him a house: and therefore thy servant hath found confidence, to pray before thee.

text.NRSV: And now, O Lord, you are God, and you have promised this good thing to your servant;
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, O Yahweh, thou, art God,—therefore hast thou spoken concerning thy servant this goodness.
text.KJV: 17:26 And now, Lord (thou art God, and hast promised this goodnesse vnto thy seruant.)
text.DRV: 17:26 Now therefore Lord thou art God: & thou hast spoken to thy servant so great benefits.

text.NRSV: now therefore may it please you to bless the house of your servant, that it may continue forever before you. For you, O Lord, have blessed and are blessed forever.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, let it please thee to bless the house of thy servant, that it may be unto times age-abiding before thee,—for, thou O Yahweh, hast blessed, and it is to be blessed unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: 17:27 Now therefore let it please thee to blesse the house of thy seruant, that it may bee before thee for euer: for thou blessest, O Lord, and it shalbe blessed for euer.
text.DRV: 17:27 And thou hast begun to bless the house of thy servant, that it be always before thee: for thee o Lord blessing it, it shall be blessed for ever.

text.NRSV: Some time afterward, David attacked the Philistines and subdued them; he took Gath and its villages from the Philistines.
text.Rotherham: And it canto to pass, after this, that David smote the Philistines, and subdued them,—and took Gath and her villages, out of the hand of the Philistines.
text.KJV: 18:1 Now after this, it came to passe, that Dauid smote the Philistines & subdued them, and tooke Gath, and her townes out of the hand of the Philistines.
text.DRV: 18:1 And it came to pass after these things, that David struck the Philisthiims, and humbled them, and took away Geth, and her daughters out of the hand of the Philisthiims,

text.NRSV: He defeated Moab, and the Moabites became subject to David and brought tribute.
text.Rotherham: And he smote Moab,—and the Moabites became servants to David, bearers of a present.
text.KJV: 18:2 And he smote Moab, and the Moabites became Dauids seruants, and brought gifts.
text.DRV: 18:2 and struck Moab; and the Moabites were made David's servants, offering him gifts.

text.NRSV: David also struck down King Hadadezer of Zobah, toward Hamath, as he went to set up a monument at the river Euphrates.
text.Rotherham: And David smote Hadarezer king of Zobah, towards Hamath,—as he went to establish his hold of the river Euphrates.
text.KJV: 18:3 ¶ And Dauid smote Hadarezer king of Zobah vnto Hamath, as hee went to stablish his dominion by the riuer Euphrates.
text.DRV: 18:3 At that time David struck Adarezer also the king of Soba of the country of Hemath, when he went on to dilate his empire as far as the river Euphrates.

text.NRSV: David took from him one thousand chariots, seven thousand cavalry, and twenty thousand foot soldiers. David hamstrung all the chariot horses, but left one hundred of them.
text.Rotherham: And David captured from him a thousand chariots, and seven thousand horsemen, and twenty thousand footmen,—and David destroyed all the chariots, saving that he reserved from them a hundred chariots.
text.KJV: 18:4 And Dauid tooke from him a thousand charets, and seuen thousand horsemen, and twentie thousand footmen: Dauid also houghed all the charet horses, but reserued of them an hundred charets.
text.DRV: 18:4 David therefore took a thousand chariots of his, & seven thousand horsemen, & twenty thousand footmen, & he houghsinewed all the chariot horses, saving an hundred chariots, which he reserved to himself.

text.NRSV: When the Arameans of Damascus came to help King Hadadezer of Zobah, David killed twenty-two thousand Arameans.
text.Rotherham: And, when the Syrians of Damascus came to help Hadarezer king of Zobah, David smote of the Syrians, twenty-two thousand men.
text.KJV: 18:5 And when the Syrians of Damascus came to helpe Hadarezer king of Zobah, Dauid slew of the Syrians two and twentie thousand men.
text.DRV: 18:5 And the Syrian also of Damascus came moreover, to give aid to Adarezer the king of Soba, but David struck also of his two & twenty thousand men.

text.NRSV: Then David put garrisons in Aram of Damascus; and the Arameans became subject to David, and brought tribute. The Lord gave victory to David wherever he went.
text.Rotherham: And David put [garrisons] in Syria of Damascus, and so it was that the Syrians became, David’s, servants, bearers of a present,—so Yahweh gave victory to David, whithersoever he went.
text.KJV: 18:6 Then Dauid put garisons in Syria Damascus, and the Syrians became Dauids seruants, and brought giftes. Thus the Lord preserued Dauid, whithersoeuer he went.
text.DRV: 18:6 And he put soldiers in Damascus, that Syria also should serve him, & bring gifts. And our Lord holp him in all things, to the which he went.

text.NRSV: David took the gold shields that were carried by the attendants of Hadadezer, and brought them to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And David took the shields of gold, that were upon the servants of Hadarezer,—and brought them to Jerusalem;
text.KJV: 18:7 And Dauid tooke the shields of golde that were on the seruants of Hadarezer, & brought them to Ierusalem.
text.DRV: 18:7 David also took the golden quivers, which the servants of Adarezer had, and he brought them into Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: From Tibhath and from Cun, cities of Hadadezer, David took a vast quantity of bronze; with it Solomon made the bronze sea and the pillars and the bronze vessels.
text.Rotherham: also, from Tibhath and from Cun, cities of Hadarezer, David took very much bronze,—therewith, did Solomon make the sea of bronze, and the pillars, and the vessels of bronze.
text.KJV: 18:8 Likewise from Tibhath, and from Chun, cities of Hadarezer, broght Dauid very much brasse, wherewith Solomon made the brasen Sea, and the pillars, and the vessels of brasse.
text.DRV: 18:8 Moreover of Thebath, and Chun, the cities of Adarezer, very much brass, of which Salomon made the brazen Sea, and Pillars, and brazen vessels.

text.NRSV: When King Tou of Hamath heard that David had defeated the whole army of King Hadadezer of Zobah,
text.Rotherham: And, when Tou, king of Hamath, heard that David had smitten all the force of Hadarezer king of Zobah,
text.KJV: 18:9 ¶ Now when Tou king of Hamath heard how Dauid had smitten all the hoste of Hadarezer king of Zobah:
text.DRV: 18:9 Which when Tou the king of Hemath had heard, to wit, that David had strucken all the army of Adarezer the king of Soba,

text.NRSV: he sent his son Hadoram to King David, to greet him and to congratulate him because he had fought against Hadadezer and defeated him. Now Hadadezer had often been at war with Tou. He sent all sorts of articles of gold, of silver, and of bronze;
text.Rotherham: he sent Hadoram his son unto King David, to ask after his welfare, and to bless him, because he had made war against Hadarezer and had smitten him, for, a man making wars against Tou, had been Hadarezer,—and [he had with him] all manner of vessels of gold and silver and bronze;
text.KJV: 18:10 Hee sent Hadoram his sonne to king Dauid, to enquire of his welfare, and to congratulate him, because hee had fought against Hadarezer, and smitten him (for Hadarezer had warre with Tou) and with him all manner of vessels of golde and siluer, and brasse.
text.DRV: 18:10 he sent Adoram his son to king David, to desire peace of him, and to congratulate him that he had strucken, and had overthrown Adarezer: for Tou was adversary to Adarezer.

text.NRSV: these also King David dedicated to the Lord, together with the silver and gold that he had carried off from all the nations, from Edom, Moab, the Ammonites, the Philistines, and Amalek.
text.Rotherham: them also, did King David hallow unto Yahweh, with the silver and the gold, that he bare away from all the nations,—from Edom, and from Moab, and from the sons of Ammon, and from the Philistines, and from Amalek.
text.KJV: 18:11 ¶ Them also king Dauid dedicated vnto the Lord, with the siluer and the golde that he brought from all these nations: from Edom, and from Moab, and from the children of Ammon, and from the Philistines, and from Amalek.
text.DRV: 18:11 But all the vessels also of gold, and silver, and brass king David consecrated to our Lord, with the silver and gold, which he had taken out of all the nations, as well of Idumea, and Moab, and the children of Ammon, as of the Philisthiims & Amalec.

text.NRSV: Abishai son of Zeruiah killed eighteen thousand Edomites in the Valley of Salt.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, Abishai, son of Zeruiah, smote Edom, in the Valley of Salt,—eighteen thousand;
text.KJV: 18:12 Moreouer, Abishai the sonne of Zeruiah, slew of the Edomites in the valley of salt, eighteene thousand.
text.DRV: 18:12 And Abisai the son of Sarvia struck Edom in the Vale of salt pits, eighteen thousand:

text.NRSV: He put garrisons in Edom; and all the Edomites became subject to David. And the Lord gave victory to David wherever he went.
text.Rotherham: and set, in Edom, garrisons, and all Edom became servants unto David,—and Yahweh gave David the victory, whithersoever he went.
text.KJV: 18:13 ¶ And he put garisons in Edom, and all the Edomites became Dauids seruants. Thus the Lord preserued Dauid whithersoeuer he went.
text.DRV: 18:13 and he appointed a garrison in Edom, that Idumea should serve David, and our Lord saved David in all things to which he went.

text.NRSV: So David reigned over all Israel; and he administered justice and equity to all his people.
text.Rotherham: So David reigned over all Israel,—and he was executing justice and righteousness for all his people;
text.KJV: 18:14 ¶ So Dauid reigned ouer all Israel, and executed iudgement and iustice among all his people.
text.DRV: 18:14 David therefore reigned over all Israel, & did judgement and justice to all his people.

text.NRSV: Joab son of Zeruiah was over the army; Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud was recorder;
text.Rotherham: and, Joab, son of Zeruiah, was over the army,—and, Jehoshaphat, son of Elihud, was recorder;
text.KJV: 18:15 And Ioab the sonne of Zeruiah was ouer the hoste, and Iehoshaphat the sonne of Ahilud, Recorder.
text.DRV: 18:15 Moreover Joab the son of Sarvia was over the army, and Josaphat the son of Ahilud commenter.

text.NRSV: Zadok son of Ahitub and Ahimelech son of Abiathar were priests; Shavsha was secretary;
text.Rotherham: and, Zadok, son of Ahitub, and Abimelech son of Abiathar, were priests,—and, Shavsha, was scribe;
text.KJV: 18:16 And Zadok the sonne of Ahitub, and Abimelech the sonne of Abiathar, were the Priests, and Shausha was Scribe.
text.DRV: 18:16 And Sadoc the son of Achitob, and Ahimelech the son of Abiathar, Priests: and Susa, Scribe.

text.NRSV: Benaiah son of Jehoiada was over the Cherethites and the Pelethites; and David’s sons were the chief officials in the service of the king.
text.Rotherham: and, Benaiah, son of Jehoiada, was over the Cherethites, and the Pelethites,—and, the sons of David, were first, in attendance on the king.
text.KJV: 18:17 And Benaiah the sonne of Iehoiada was ouer the Cherethites, and the Pelethites: and the sonnes of Dauid were chiefe about the king.
text.DRV: 18:17 Banaias also the son of Joiada over the legions Cerethi, and Phelethi: moreover the sons of David the chief at the king's hand.

text.NRSV: Some time afterward, King Nahash of the Ammonites died, and his son succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, after this, that Nahash king of the sons of Ammon died,—and Hanun his son reigned in his stead.
text.KJV: 19:1 Now it came to passe after this, that Nahash the King of the children of Ammon dyed, & his sonne reigned in his stead.
text.DRV: 19:1 And it chanced that Naas the king of the children of Ammon died, and his son reigned for him.

text.NRSV: David said, “I will deal loyally with Hanun son of Nahash, for his father dealt loyally with me.” So David sent messengers to console him concerning his father. When David’s servants came to Hanun in the land of the Ammonites, to console him,
text.Rotherham: Then said David—I will show lovingkindness unto Hanun, son of Nahash,—for his father shewed unto me lovingkindness. So David sent messengers to console him concerning his father,—and the servants of David came into the land of the sons of Ammon unto Hanun, to console him.
text.KJV: 19:2 And Dauid sayde, I will shewe kindnesse vnto Hanun the sonne of Nahash, because his father shewed kindnesse to mee. And Dauid sent messengers to comfort him concerning his father. So the seruants of Dauid came into the land of the children of Ammon, to Hanun, to comfort him.
text.DRV: 19:2 And David said: I will do mercy with Hanon the son of Naas: for his father hath done me pleasure. And David sent messengers to comfort him upon the death of his father. Who when they were come into the land of the children of Ammon, to comfort Hanon,

text.NRSV: the officials of the Ammonites said to Hanun, “Do you imagine, because David has sent consolers to you, that he is honoring your father? Have not his servants come to you to search and to overthrow and to spy out the land?”
text.Rotherham: Then said the rulers of the sons of Ammon unto Hanun, Doth David honour thy father in thine eyes, that he hath sent to thee comforters? Is it not, for the sake of exploring and overthrowing, and spying out the land, that his servants have come to thee?
text.KJV: 19:3 But the Princes of the children of Ammon sayde to Hanun, Thinkest thou that Dauid doeth honour thy father, that he hath sent comforters vnto thee? Are not his seruants come vnto thee for to search, and to ouerthrow, and to spie out the land?
text.DRV: 19:3 the Princes of the children of Ammon said to Hanon: Thou thinkest perhaps, that David for honour sake toward thy father hath sent some that should comfort thee; neither markest thou, that his servants are come to thee to espy, and seek out, and search thy land.

text.NRSV: So Hanun seized David’s servants, shaved them, cut off their garments in the middle at their hips, and sent them away;
text.Rotherham: So Hanun took the servants of David, and shaved them, and cut off their upper garments in the middle, as far as their parts of shame,—and let them go.
text.KJV: 19:4 Wherefore Hanun tooke Dauids seruants, and shaued them, and cut off their garments in the middest, hard by their buttockes, and sent them away.
text.DRV: 19:4 Therefore Hanon made the servants of David bald, and shaved them, and cut away their coats from the buttocks to the feet, and sent them away.

text.NRSV: and they departed. When David was told about the men, he sent messengers to them, for they were greatly ashamed. The king said, “Stay at Jericho until your beards have grown, and then return.”
text.Rotherham: And people went and told David, concerning the men, so he sent to meet them, because the men were greatly ashamed,—and the king said—Tarry at Jericho, until your beards be grown, and then return.
text.KJV: 19:5 Then there went certeine, and told Dauid, how the men were serued, and hee sent to meet them (for the men were greatly ashamed) and the King sayde, Tary at Iericho vntill your beards be growen, and then returne.
text.DRV: 19:5 Who when they were gone, and had sent word to David, he sent to meet them (for they had sustained great reproach) and commanded them to tarry in Jericho, till their beard grew, and then they should return.

text.NRSV: When the Ammonites saw that they had made themselves odious to David, Hanun and the Ammonites sent a thousand talents of silver to hire chariots and cavalry from Mesopotamia, from Aram-maacah and from Zobah.
