text.NRSV: Riches and honor come from you, and you rule over all. In your hand are power and might; and it is in your hand to make great and to give strength to all.
text.Rotherham: and, riches and honour, are from before thee, and, thou, art ruling over all, and, in thy hand, are power and might,—and, in thy hand, it is, to give greatness and strength unto any.
text.KJV: 29:12 Both riches, and honour come of thee, and thou reignest ouer all, and in thine hand is power and might, and in thine hand it is to make great, and to giue strength vnto all.
text.DRV: 29:12 Thine are riches and thine is glory: thou hast dominion over all, in thy hand is power and might: in thy hand greatness, and the empire of all things.

text.NRSV: And now, our God, we give thanks to you and praise your glorious name.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, O our God, we are giving thanks unto thee,—and offering praise, unto thy beautiful Name.
text.KJV: 29:13 Now therefore, our God, wee thanke thee, and praise thy glorious Name.
text.DRV: 29:13 Now therefore our God we confess to thee, and we praise thy glorious name.

text.NRSV: “But who am I, and what is my people, that we should be able to make this freewill offering? For all things come from you, and of your own have we given you.
text.Rotherham: And yet, who am, I, and who are my people, that we should be able to offer willingly like this? for, from thee, is the whole, and, out of thine own hand, have we given unto thee;
text.KJV: 29:14 But who am I, and what is my people, that we should be able to offer so willingly after this sort? for all things come of thee, and of thine owne haue we giuen thee.
text.DRV: 29:14 Who am I, and what is my people, that we can promise thee all these things? All are thine: and things that we received of thy hand, we have given thee.

text.NRSV: For we are strangers before you, and sojourners, as were all our ancestors; our days on the earth are like a shadow, and there is no hope.
text.Rotherham: for, sojourners, are we before thee, and strangers, like all our fathers,—like a shadow, are our days upon the earth, and there is no hope.
text.KJV: 29:15 For we are strangers before thee, and soiourners, as were all our fathers: Our dayes on the earth are as a shadow, and there is none abiding.
text.DRV: 29:15 For we are pilgrims before thee, and strangers, as all our fathers. Our days are as a shadow upon the earth, and there is no abiding.

text.NRSV: O Lord our God, all this abundance that we have provided for building you a house for your holy name comes from your hand and is all your own.
text.Rotherham: O Yahweh our God, all this abundance which we have prepared, to build thee a house for thy holy Name, out of thine own hand, it is, and, thine, is the whole.
text.KJV: 29:16 O Lord our God, all this store that we haue prepared to build thee an house for thine holy Name, commeth of thine hand, and is all thine owne.
text.DRV: 29:16 Lord our God, all this plenty, which we have prepared that a house might be built to thy holy name, is of thy hand, and all things are thine.

text.NRSV: I know, my God, that you search the heart, and take pleasure in uprightness; in the uprightness of my heart I have freely offered all these things, and now I have seen your people, who are present here, offering freely and joyously to you.
text.Rotherham: But I know, O my God, that, thou, art proving the heart, and, with uprightness, wilt thou be pleased,—I, in the uprightness of my heart, have willingly offered all these things, now, therefore, thy people who are found here, have I seen with joy, offering willingly unto thee.
text.KJV: 29:17 I know also, my God, that thou triest the heart, and hast pleasure in vprightnesse. As for me, in the vprightnes of mine heart I haue willingly offered all these things: and now haue I seene with ioy, thy people which are present here, to offer willingly vnto thee.
text.DRV: 29:17 I know my God that thou provest the hearts, and lovest simplicity, wherefore I also in the simplicity of my heart, gladly have offered all these things: and thy people, which is here found, I have seen with great joy offer thee donaries.

text.NRSV: O Lord, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, our ancestors, keep forever such purposes and thoughts in the hearts of your people, and direct their hearts toward you.
text.Rotherham: O Yahweh, God of Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, our fathers, oh guard this age-abidingly, as the devised purpose of the heart of thy people,—and establish thou their heart, unto thyself.
text.KJV: 29:18 O Lord God of Abraham, Isaac and of Israel our fathers, keepe this for euer in the imagination of the thoughts of the heart of thy people, and prepare their heart vnto thee:
text.DRV: 29:18 Lord God of Abraham, and Isaac, and Israel our fathers, keep for ever this will of their heart, and let this mind remain always to the honour of thee.

text.NRSV: Grant to my son Solomon that with single mind he may keep your commandments, your decrees, and your statutes, performing all of them, and that he may build the temple for which I have made provision.”
text.Rotherham: Also, unto Solomon my son, give thou an undivided heart, to keep thy commandments, thy testimonies, and thy statutes,—to do the whole, and to build the palace for which I have prepared.
text.KJV: 29:19 And giue vnto Solomon my sonne a perfect heart to keepe thy Commaundements, thy testimonies, and thy statutes, and to doe all these things, and to build the pallace, for the which I haue made prouision.
text.DRV: 29:19 To Salomon also my son give a perfect heart, that he keep thy commandments, thy testimonies, and thy ceremonies, and do all things: and build the house, the expenses whereof I have prepared.

text.NRSV: Then David said to the whole assembly, “Bless the Lord your God.” And all the assembly blessed the Lord, the God of their ancestors, and bowed their heads and bent low before the Lord and the king.
text.Rotherham: Then said David, unto all the convocation, Bless, I beseech you, Yahweh your God. So all the convocation blessed Yahweh, the God of their fathers, and did bend their heads and bow themselves down unto Yahweh, and unto the king;
text.KJV: 29:20 ¶ And Dauid said to all the Congregation: Nowe blesse the Lord your God. And all the Congregation blessed the Lord God of their fathers, and bowed downe their heads, and worshipped the Lord, and the King.
text.DRV: 29:20 And David commanded the whole assembly: Bless ye our Lord God. And all the assembly blessed our Lord the God of their fathers: and they bowed themselves, & adored God, and then the king.

text.NRSV: On the next day they offered sacrifices and burnt offerings to the Lord, a thousand bulls, a thousand rams, and a thousand lambs, with their drink offerings, and sacrifices in abundance for all Israel.
text.Rotherham: and sacrificed unto Yahweh sacrifices, and caused to go up ascending-offerings unto Yahweh, on the morrow of that day, a thousand bullocks, a thousand rams, a thousand young sheep, and the drink-offerings thereof,—and sacrifices in abundance, for all Israel.
text.KJV: 29:21 And they sacrificed sacrifices vnto the Lord, & offered burnt offerings vnto the Lord on the morrow after that day, euen a thousand bullockes, a thousand rams, and a thousand lambes, with their drinke offerings, and sacrifices in abundance for all Israel:
text.DRV: 29:21 And they immolated victim to our Lord: and they offered holocausts the day following, oxen a thousand, rams a thousand, lambs a thousand with their libaments, and with all rite most aboundantly for all Israel.

text.NRSV: They ate and drank before the Lord on that day with great joy. They made Solomon son of David king a second time; they anointed him as the Lord’s prince, and Zadok as priest.
text.Rotherham: And so they did eat and drink before Yahweh on that day, with great joy,—and they, the second time, made Solomon son of David king, and anointed him unto Yahweh to be chief ruler, and Zadok to be priest.
text.KJV: 29:22 And did eate and drinke before the Lord on that day with great gladnesse, and they made Solomon the sonne of Dauid King the second time, and anointed him vnto the Lord to be the chiefe gouernour, and Zadok to be Priest.
text.DRV: 29:22 And they did eat, and drink before our Lord in that day with great joy. And they anointed the second time Salomon the son of David. And they anointed him to our Lord for the Prince, and Sadoc for the high Priest.

text.NRSV: Then Solomon sat on the throne of the Lord as king instead of his father David; he prospered, and all Israel obeyed him.
text.Rotherham: So Solomon took his seat upon the throne of Yahweh, as king, instead of David his father, and prospered,—and all Israel, hearkened unto him.
text.KJV: 29:23 Then Solomon sate on the throne of the Lord, as king in stead of Dauid his father, and prospered, and all Israel obeyed him.
text.DRV: 29:23 And Salomon sat upon the throne of our Lord as king for David his father, and he pleased all: and all Israel obeyed him.

text.NRSV: All the leaders and the mighty warriors, and also all the sons of King David, pledged their allegiance to King Solomon.
text.Rotherham: And, all the rulers, and the heroes, yea moreover all the sons of King David, gave a hand, under Solomon the king.
text.KJV: 29:24 And all the princes and the mightie men, and all the sonnes likewise of king Dauid, submitted themselues vnto Solomon the King.
text.DRV: 29:24 Yea and all the princes, and men of might, and all the sons of king David gave their hand, and were subject to Salomon the king.

text.NRSV: The Lord highly exalted Solomon in the sight of all Israel, and bestowed upon him such royal majesty as had not been on any king before him in Israel.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh magnified Solomon, exceedingly, before the eyes of all Israel,—and gave unto him a royal majesty, that had not been on any king before him, over Israel.
text.KJV: 29:25 And the Lord magnified Solomon exceedingly in the sight of all Israel, and bestowed vpon him such royal maiestie, as had not bene on any king before him in Israel.
text.DRV: 29:25 Our Lord therefore magnified Salomon over all Israel: & gave him the glory of a Kingdom, such as no king of Israel had before him.

text.NRSV: Thus David son of Jesse reigned over all Israel.
text.Rotherham: Thus, David son of Jesse, reigned over all Israel;
text.KJV: 29:26 ¶ Thus Dauid the sonne of Iesse, reigned ouer all Israel.
text.DRV: 29:26 David therefore the son of Isai reigned over all Israel.

text.NRSV: The period that he reigned over Israel was forty years; he reigned seven years in Hebron, and thirty-three years in Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: and, the days that he reigned over Israel, were forty years,—in Hebron reigned he seven years, and in Jerusalem, reigned he, thirty-three.
text.KJV: 29:27 And the time that he reigned ouer Israel, was fourtie yeeres. Seuen yeeres reigned hee in Hebron, and thirtie and three yeeres reigned hee in Ierusalem.
text.DRV: 29:27 And the days that he reigned over Israel, were forty years: in Hebron he reigned seven years, and in Jerusalem three & thirty years.

text.NRSV: He died in a good old age, full of days, riches, and honor; and his son Solomon succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And he died in a good old age, satisfied with days, riches and honour,—and Solomon his son reigned in his stead.
text.KJV: 29:28 And he died in a good old age, full of dayes, riches and honour: and Solomon his sonne reigned in his stead.
text.DRV: 29:28 And he died in a good old age, full of days, and riches, & glory. And Salomon his son reigned for him.

text.NRSV: Now the acts of King David, from first to last, are written in the Annals of the Prophet Samuel, and in the Annals of the Prophet Nathan, and in the Annals of the Prophet Gad,
text.Rotherham: Now, the acts of David the king, first and last, behold them! written in the records of Samuel the seer, and in the records of Nathan the prophet, and in the records of Gad the seer:
text.KJV: 29:29 Now the acts of Dauid the King first and last, behold, they are written in the booke of Samuel the Seer, and in the booke of Nathan the Prophet, and in the booke of Gad the Seer,
text.DRV: 29:29 But the acts of king David the first and the last are written in the Book of Samuel the Seer, and in the Book of Nathan the Prophet, and in the Volume of Gad the Seer:

text.NRSV: with accounts of all his rule and his might and of the events that befell him and Israel and all the kingdoms of the earth.
text.Rotherham: with all his reign, and his might,—and the times which passed over him, and over Israel, and over all the kingdoms of the lands.
text.KJV: 29:30 With all his reigne and his might, and the times that went ouer him, and ouer Israel, and ouer all the kingdomes of the countreys.
text.DRV: 29:30 and of all his Kingdom, and strength, and of the times, that passed under him, either in Israel, or in all the Kingdoms of the earth.
